# Setup

```{r}
inla_df = readRDS("~/work/causal-wildfire-smoke-2-duplicate/inla_df_2.11.rds")


inla_df = st_drop_geometry(inla_df)

inla_df$nonsmoke_pm25 = inla_df$pm25 - inla_df$monthly_total_pm25
inla_df$nonsmoke_pm25[inla_df$nonsmoke_pm25 < 0] = 0
```

```{r}
  theme_bw()
```

## Creating cumulative covariate columns (with memory property)

```{r,warning=FALSE,echo=FALSE}
create_rolling_agg_var <- function(data, variable_name, county_col = "NAME", 
                               lags = c(3, 6), n_periods = 72, 
                               agg_fun = sum, agg_name = "cum") {
  
  # Initialize result lists
  results <- list()
  
  # Create a column for each lag
  for (lag in lags) {
    results[[paste0(agg_name, "_", variable_name, lag)]] <- c()
  }
  
  # Loop through each county
  for (i in 1:length(counties)) {
    
    # Extract county data
    county_data <- data %>% 
      filter(!!sym(county_col) == counties[i]) %>% 
      dplyr::select(!!sym(variable_name)) %>% 
      st_drop_geometry()
    
    county_values <- county_data[[variable_name]]
    
    # Calculate rolling aggregations for each lag
    for (lag in lags) {
      col_name <- paste0(agg_name, "_", variable_name, "_", lag)
      
      # Initialize county aggregation vector
      county_agg <- numeric(n_periods)
      
      # Fill initial values (first 'lag' periods)
      county_agg[1:(lag-1)] <- county_values[1:(lag-1)]
      
      # Calculate rolling aggregations
      for (j in lag:n_periods) {
        county_agg[j] <- agg_fun(county_values[(j - lag + 1):j])
      }
      
      # Append to results
      results[[col_name]] <- c(results[[col_name]], county_agg)
    }
  }
  
  # Add results to original dataframe
  for (col_name in names(results)) {
    data[[col_name]] <- results[[col_name]]
  }
  
  return(data)
}


#Treatment (cumulative sum)
inla_df <- create_rolling_agg_var(
  data = inla_df,
  variable_name = "monthly_total_pm25",
  county_col = "NAME",
  lags = c(2,3),
  n_periods = 72,
  agg_fun = sum,
  agg_name = "cum"
)

#Precipitation (cumulative sum)
inla_df <- create_rolling_agg_var(
  data = inla_df,
  variable_name = "total_precip",
  county_col = "NAME",
  lags = c(2,3),
  n_periods = 72,
  agg_fun = sum,
  agg_name = "cum"
)

#Temperature (mean)
inla_df <- create_rolling_agg_var(
  data = inla_df,
  variable_name = "avg_air_temp",
  county_col = "NAME",
  lags = c(2,3),
  n_periods = 72,
  agg_fun = mean,
  agg_name = "avg"
)

#Humidity (mean)
inla_df <- create_rolling_agg_var(
  data = inla_df,
  variable_name = "avg_dew_point",
  county_col = "NAME",
  lags = c(2,3),
  n_periods = 72,
  agg_fun = mean,
  agg_name = "avg"
)

#AQI (max)
inla_df <- create_rolling_agg_var(
  data = inla_df,
  variable_name = "AQI",
  county_col = "NAME",
  lags = c(2,3),
  n_periods = 72,
  agg_fun = max,
  agg_name = "max"
)

#Wind speed (max)
inla_df <- create_rolling_agg_var(
  data = inla_df,
  variable_name = "avg_wind_speed",
  county_col = "NAME",
  lags = c(2,3),
  n_periods = 72,
  agg_fun = max,
  agg_name = "max"
)
```

## Explore significant lags of treatment:

```{r}
test <- inla_df %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  mutate(
    monthly_total_pm25_lag1 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 1),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag2 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 2),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag3 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 3),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag4 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 4),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag5 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 5),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag6 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 6),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag7 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 7),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag8 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 8),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag9 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 9),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag10 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 10),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag11 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 11),
    monthly_total_pm25_lag12 = lag(monthly_total_pm25, 12)
  ) %>% ungroup()

model = inla(formula = formula,family = "nbinomial",data = test,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,                                      return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.fixed = list(prec = 50),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))


model$summary.fixed[71:82,3:5]
```

## Create lags for each confounder and exposure:

```{r}
covariates <- c("total_precip", "avg_air_temp", "avg_dew_point", 
                "avg_wind_speed","lead","co","so2","no2","o3",
                "pm10","nonsmoke_pm25","percent_ili")

inla_df <- inla_df %>%
  group_by(ID)

for (var in covariates) {
  for (lag_num in 1:6) {
    new_col <- paste0(var, "_lag", lag_num)
    inla_df <- inla_df %>%
      mutate(!!new_col := lag(.data[[var]], lag_num))
  }
}

inla_df <- inla_df %>% ungroup()
```

```{r}
#Add in lags of treatment e.g. lags 1-6
inla_df <- inla_df %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  mutate(
    cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag1 = lag(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 1),
    cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag2 = lag(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 2),
    cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag3 = lag(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 3),
    cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag4 = lag(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 4),
    cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag5 = lag(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 5),
    cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag6 = lag(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 6)
  ) %>% ungroup()
```

# Simplify clusters to quintiles of SDI score instead of SKATER

```{r}
# Calculate quintile breakpoints
SDI_groups = quantile(inla_df$SDI, c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8))

# Calculate average SDI per county
avg_SDI = apply(SDI_df, 2, mean)
avg_SDI_df = data.frame(counties, avg_SDI)

# Assign group labels based on quintiles
grouplabels = rep(NA, nrow(avg_SDI_df))  # Initialize vector

grouplabels[avg_SDI <= SDI_groups[1]] = 1
grouplabels[avg_SDI > SDI_groups[1] & avg_SDI <= SDI_groups[2]] = 2
grouplabels[avg_SDI > SDI_groups[2] & avg_SDI <= SDI_groups[3]] = 3
grouplabels[avg_SDI > SDI_groups[3] & avg_SDI <= SDI_groups[4]] = 4
grouplabels[avg_SDI > SDI_groups[4]] = 5

# Add to dataframe
avg_SDI_df$Group = grouplabels
group_labels = avg_SDI_df[,c(1,3)]

# inla_df = inla_df %>% left_join(group_labels,by = c("NAME" = "counties"))
# inla_df$Group = factor(inla_df$Group)
```

# Modeling treatment

## Estimate a spatial dependence structure

We can either use the observed neighborhood structure (queen adjacency) or estimate a graph with HUGE

```{r,eval=FALSE}
#Identify neighborhood list for counties 
CA_nb = poly2nb(CA_spdf)

#summary(CA_nb)

# plot(CA_spdf, main = "With queen")
# plot(CA_nb, coords = coordinates(CA_spdf), col="blue", add = TRUE)

#Calculate edge costs (dissimilarity matrix) based on Euclidean distance 
costs <- nbcosts(CA_nb, data = covariates_scale)

###Get adjacency matrix using nb2mat() (SEPARATE STEP FOR INLA)
adj = nb2mat(CA_nb,style = "B")

#Style means the coding scheme style used to create the weighting matrix 
# B: basic binary coding scheme
# W: row standardized coding scheme 
# C: globally standardized coding scheme  
# U: values of C / number of neighbors 
# S: variance stabilizing coding scheme 
```

```{r,eval=FALSE}
huge_input = as.matrix(score_scaled)
#huge_input = as.matrix(SDI_df)

#Graph learning w HUGE
out.glasso = huge(huge_input,lambda = seq(0.95,0.05,by=-0.05),method="glasso")

glasso.stars = huge.select(out.glasso,criterion = "stars",stars.thresh = 0.1)
glasso.ric = huge.select(out.glasso,criterion = "ric")
glasso.ebic = huge.select(out.glasso,criterion = "ebic")

plot(glasso.stars)
plot(glasso.ric)
plot(glasso.ebic)

huge.est = glasso.ebic$refit
#huge.est

degree_connectivity = data.frame(colSums(huge.est))
colnames(degree_connectivity) = "node_connections"
degree_connectivity = cbind(c(1:num_counties),degree_connectivity)
```

### Transforming estimated adjacency matrix to graph filter H

The code below takes the adjacency matrix estimated in the previous step and transforms it into a graph filter H. The steps are explained in Antonian et al 2019 (Gareth Peters' paper). The cutoff transformation is applied to the eigenvalues of the graph Laplacian.

```{r}
#A = as.matrix(huge.est)
A = as.matrix(adj)

p = nrow(A)

#obtain graph Laplacian L
D = diag(p)
for (i in 1:p){
  d = sum(A[,i])
  D[i,i] = d
}

L = D - A

#eigendecomposition of L
Ldecomp = eigen(L)
U = as.matrix(Ldecomp$vectors)
Lambdas = Ldecomp$values

#test
#U %*% (diag(p)*Lambdas) %*% t(U)

#Function implementing cutoff tranform for eigenvalues 
# cutoff.transform = function(lambdas,q){
#   transformed = c()
#   cutoff = quantile(lambdas,q)
#   for (i in lambdas){
#     if(i <= cutoff){
#       transformed = c(transformed,i)
#     }
#     else{
#       transformed = c(transformed,0)
#     }
#   }
  
#   return(transformed)
# }

#Corrected
cutoff.transform = function(lambdas,q){
  transformed = c()
  N = length(lambdas)
  for (i in 1:N){
    if(i/N <= q){
      transformed = c(transformed,1)
    }
    else{
      transformed = c(transformed,0)
    }
  }
  
  return(transformed)
}

#quantile(Lambdas,2/3)
#transformed.L = cutoff.transform(Lambdas,1/3)
transformed.L = cutoff.transform(Lambdas,2/3)
#transformed.L = cutoff.transform(Lambdas,0.99)
eta.L = diag(p)*transformed.L

#obtain graph filter
H = U %*% eta.L %*% t(U)
```

Heatmap of resulting graph filter:

```{r}
matrix_heatmap = function(
  matrix, 
  title = "", 
  legend_title = "Covariance", 
  xlab = "Entry j",
  ylab = "Entry i",
  gradient_zones = c(0, 0.5, 0.999),
  save_path = "/home/jovyan/work/important-plots",
  prefix = "",           
  filename = "heatmap.png", # base filename
  text_scale = 2){
  r = nrow(matrix)
  c = ncol(matrix)
  
  df = as_tibble(cbind(expand.grid(rev(seq_len(r)), seq_len(c)), c(matrix))) %>%
    setNames(c("row", "col", "value"))
  
  df$value[df$value == 1] = 0.999
  
  x_min = min(df$row) - 0.5
  x_max = max(df$row) + 0.5
  y_min = min(df$col) - 0.5
  y_max = max(df$col) + 0.5
  
  plot = ggplot(df, mapping = aes(x = row, y = col, fill = value)) +
    geom_tile() +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = x_min, xmax = x_max, ymin = y_min, ymax = y_max), 
              fill = NA, color = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors = c("white", "lightblue", "darkblue"),
      values = rescale(gradient_zones),
      limits = c(0, 0.99),
      oob = squish,
      name = legend_title
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = c(1,c),
      labels = paste(c(1,c)),  # reversed because we used rev() earlier
      name = xlab
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = c(r,1),
      labels = paste(c(1,r)),
      name = ylab
    ) +
    ggtitle(title) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11 * text_scale) +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 30, b = 10, l = 10),
      legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5),  # center x labels
      panel.grid = element_blank()  # remove grid lines
    )

    # ggsave(
    #   filename = paste0(prefix, filename),
    #   plot = plot,
    #   path = save_path,
    #   width = 8,
    #   height = 6,
    #   dpi = 600
    # )

  return(plot)
}

matrix_heatmap(H,prefix = "gfilter-")
```

## Estimate a temporal dependence structure

### Calculating covariate similarity kernel K_cov

```{r}
cov_similarity_kernel = function(cov_data,time_span,rho_rbf,rho_periodic,sigma2,additive = TRUE){
  K_cov = matrix(0,nrow=time_span,ncol=time_span)
  i = 1
  j = 1
  
  for(t1 in 1:time_span){
    for (t2 in 1:time_span){
      A = cov_data %>% filter(time == t1) 
      B = cov_data %>% filter(time == t2) 
      # AQIa = unique(A$AQI)
      # AQIb = unique(B$AQI)
      
      diff = (A$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2-B$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)^2
      
      if(additive == TRUE){
        K_cov[i,j] = (exp(- (mean(diff)) 
           / (2*rho_rbf)) + exp(- (2*sin(sum(abs(diff))*pi/12)^2)
           / (rho_periodic))) * sigma2
      } else{
        K_cov[i,j] = exp(- (mean(diff))
                 / (2*rho_rbf)) * exp(- (2*sin(sum(abs(diff))*pi/12)^2)
                 / (rho_periodic)) * sigma2
      }
      
      j = j+1
    }
    
    j = 1
    i = i+1
  }
  
  return(K_cov)
}
```

## Calculating simple time kernel K_time

```{r}
time_rbf_kernel = function(time_span,rho_rbf,rho_periodic,sigma2){
  K_time = matrix(NA,nrow = time_span, ncol = time_span)

  for (i in 1:time_span){
    for (j in 1:time_span){
      K_time[i,j] = exp(- (abs(i-j)^2) / (2*rho_rbf)) * sigma2 #JUST RBF
      
      # K_time[i,j] = exp(- (2*sin(sum(abs(i-j))*pi/12)^2) #JUST PERIODIC
      #          / (rho_periodic)) * sigma2
    }
  }

  return(K_time)
}

time_kernel = function(time_span,rho_rbf,rho_periodic,sigma2,additive = TRUE){
  K_time = matrix(NA,nrow = time_span, ncol = time_span)

  for (i in 1:time_span){
    for (j in 1:time_span){
      
      if(additive == TRUE){
        K_time[i,j] = (exp(- (abs(i-j)^2) / (2*rho_rbf)) + exp(- (2*sin(sum(abs(i-j))*pi/12)^2)
               / (rho_periodic))) * sigma2
      } else{
        K_time[i,j] = (exp(- (abs(i-j)^2) / (2*rho_rbf)) * exp(- (2*sin(sum(abs(i-j))*pi/12)^2)
                 / (rho_periodic))) * sigma2
      }
        
    }
  }

  return(K_time)
}

Krbf = time_rbf_kernel(time_span = length(unique(inla_df$time)),rho_rbf = 10, rho_periodic = 1,sigma2 = 1)
Klp = time_kernel(time_span = length(unique(inla_df$time)),rho_rbf = 0.01, rho_periodic = 1,sigma2 = 1)

matrix_heatmap(Krbf,prefix = "Krbf-")
# matrix_heatmap(Klp,prefix = "Klp-")
```

### Ensuring precision matrix is not computationally singular, so we jitter eigenvalues, using reciprocal condition number as constraint

```{r}
desingularize = function(covmatrix,threshold = 1e-2,increment = 0.01){
  
  tracker = 0
  
  while (rcond(covmatrix) <= threshold){
    #Perform spectral decomposition
    ev = eigen(covmatrix)
    L = ev$values
    V = ev$vectors
    
    # #Add a little noise to eigenvalues to bring away from 0
    L = L + increment
    
    # #Add 0.01 to eigenvalues in bottom five percentile to bring away from 0
    # cutoff = quantile(abs(L),0.05)
    # L[which(abs(L) < cutoff)] = L[which(abs(L) < cutoff)] + 0.01
    
    #Calculate new precision matrix 
    covmatrix = V %*% diag(L) %*% t(V)
    
    tracker = tracker + 1
  }
  
  results_list = list(covmatrix,tracker)
  #sprintf("%s iterations of desingularizer applied",tracker)
  return(results_list)
}

# test = desingularize(K_time)
```

## Compute kernels and then spatiotemporal covariance matrices

```{r,cache=TRUE}
K_df = inla_df %>% dplyr::select(time,cum_monthly_total_pm25_2) %>% st_drop_geometry()

###Treatment (34.674340,93.99833,5.052856) WAIC = 42756.57, (0.0185147378, 0.0695663638, 4.6295870) WAIC = 42535.99
K_cov1 = cov_similarity_kernel(K_df,time_span = 72,rho_rbf = 34.674340,rho_periodic = 93.99833,
                               sigma2 = 5.052856, additive = FALSE) 

K_cov1.2 = cov_similarity_kernel(K_df,time_span = 72,rho_rbf = 0.0185147378,rho_periodic = 0.0695663638,
                                 sigma2 = 4.6295870, additive = TRUE) 

# K_cov1_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(K_cov1.2,title = "")

#Calculate trace norm of gram matrix
K_cov1_weight = norm((1/72)*K_cov1.2,type = "F")

#Calculate proposed kernel
covGP1_treatment = kronecker((K_cov1.2/72),(H^2/58))

#Need to ensure precision matrix is not computationally singular i.e det > 0
covGP_jittered = desingularize(covGP1_treatment,threshold = 1e-5,increment = 0.2)
covGP1_treatment = covGP_jittered[[1]]
num_jitters = covGP_jittered[[2]]

inv_covGP1_treatment = solve(covGP1_treatment)

#Heatmap of resulting inv_covGP1
# inv_covGP1_treatment_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(inv_covGP1_treatment,title = "")



##Response (58.998325,24.22638,37.477465) WAIC = 19948.19, (0.0568448990, 0.0120379678, 3.9571684) WAIC = 20046.18
K_cov2 = cov_similarity_kernel(K_df,time_span = 72,rho_rbf = 58.998325,rho_periodic = 24.22638,
                               sigma2 = 37.477465, additive = FALSE) 

K_cov2.2 = cov_similarity_kernel(K_df,time_span = 72,rho_rbf = 0.0568448990,rho_periodic = 0.0120379678,
                                 sigma2 = 3.9571684, additive = TRUE) 

matrix_heatmap(K_cov2.2,title = "",prefix = "Kcov-")

covGP1_response = kronecker((K_cov2.2/72),(H^2/58))

#Need to ensure precision matrix is not computationally singular i.e det > 0
covGP_jittered = desingularize(covGP1_response,threshold = 1e-5,increment = 0.2)
covGP1_response = covGP_jittered[[1]]
num_jitters = covGP_jittered[[2]]

inv_covGP1_response = solve(covGP1_response)

#Heatmap of resulting inv_covGP2 
# inv_covGP1_response_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(inv_covGP1_response,title = "",prefix = "outcome-model-")
```

```{r,cache=TRUE}
#Calculating gram matrix K_time

###Treatment (9.364325,32.45866,24.731415) WAIC = 42753.99, (0.072685962, 0.3132679, 1.6001799) WAIC = 42535.99
K_time1 = time_kernel(time_span = length(unique(inla_df$time)),rho_rbf = 9.364325, 
                      rho_periodic = 32.45866,sigma2 = 24.731415, additive = FALSE)

K_time1.2 = time_kernel(time_span = length(unique(inla_df$time)),rho_rbf = 0.072685962, 
                      rho_periodic = 0.3132679,sigma2 = 1.6001799, additive = TRUE)

#Heatmap of resulting K 
K_time1_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(K_time1.2,title = "",prefix = "Krbf2-")

#Calculate trace norm of gram matrix
K_time1_weight = norm((1/72)*K_time1.2,type = "F")

#Calculate proposed kernel
covGP2_treatment = kronecker((K_time1.2/72),(H^2/58))
covGP2_treatment2 = kronecker((Krbf/72),(H^2/58))

# covGP2_treatment_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(covGP2_treatment2,title = "",prefix = "treatment-cov-")

#Need to ensure precision matrix is not computationally singular i.e det > 0
covGP_jittered = desingularize(covGP2_treatment,threshold = 1e-5,increment = 0.2)
covGP2_treatment = covGP_jittered[[1]]
num_jitters = covGP_jittered[[2]]

covGP_jittered = desingularize(covGP2_treatment2,threshold = 1e-5,increment = 0.2)
covGP2_treatment2 = covGP_jittered[[1]]
num_jitters = covGP_jittered[[2]]

inv_covGP2_treatment = solve(covGP2_treatment)
inv_covGP2_treatment2 = solve(covGP2_treatment2)

#Heatmap of resulting inv_covGP2 
# inv_covGP2_treatment_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(inv_covGP2_treatment2,title = "",prefix = "treatment-prec-")

###Response (21.611833,50.17043,54.861003) WAIC = 19806.30 , (0.008535596, 6.8016543, 4.6925669) WAIC = 20086.22
K_time2 = time_kernel(time_span = length(unique(inla_df$time)),rho_rbf = 21.611833, 
                      rho_periodic = 50.17043, sigma2 = 54.861003, additive = FALSE)

K_time2.2 = time_kernel(time_span = length(unique(inla_df$time)),rho_rbf = 100, 
                      rho_periodic = 0.2, sigma2 = 54.861003, additive = FALSE)

K_time2.3 = time_kernel(time_span = length(unique(inla_df$time)),rho_rbf = 0.072685962, 
                      rho_periodic = 0.3132679,sigma2 = 1.6001799, additive = TRUE)



covGP2_response = kronecker((K_time2.3/72),(H^2/58))

#Heatmap of resulting K 
# K_time2_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(K_time2.3,title = "K_time heatmap")

#Calculate trace norm of gram matrix
K_time_weight = norm((1/72)*K_time2.3,type = "F")

#Calculate proposed kernel
covGP2_response = kronecker((K_time2.3/72),(H^2/58))

# covGP2_response_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(covGP2_response,title = "",prefix = "outcome-cov-")

#Need to ensure precision matrix is not computationally singular i.e det > 0
covGP_jittered = desingularize(covGP2_response,threshold = 1e-5,increment = 0.2)
covGP2_response = covGP_jittered[[1]]
num_jitters = covGP_jittered[[2]]

inv_covGP2_response = solve(covGP2_response)
# inv_covGP2_response = as(kronecker(solve(K_time2.2/72), solve(H^2/58)), "sparseMatrix")


#Heatmap of resulting inv_covGP2 
# inv_covGP2_response_heatmap = matrix_heatmap(inv_covGP2_response,title = "",prefix = "outcome-prec-")
```

## Next fit two KGR models with an estimated graph filter and a temporal kernel

Split data to in and out of sample:

```{r}
inla_df_train = inla_df[inla_df$Year < 2019,]
inla_df_test = inla_df[inla_df$Year == 2019,]
```

### Using K_cov

```{r,warning=FALSE}
kgr_model1_treatment = function(dataset,link=1){
  
  ###Fit INLA model 
  kgr_formula1 = (cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 + 0.01) ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + month + lead + co + so2 + no2 + o3 + pm10 + nonsmoke_pm25 + total_precip + avg_air_temp + avg_dew_point + avg_wind_speed + f(id2,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = inv_covGP1_treatment)
  
  model = inla(formula = kgr_formula1,family = "lognormal",
               data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                  control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,
                                         return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                    control.family = list(hyper = list(prec = list(
                      prior = "gaussian",param = c(1, 0.5)))), #sigma ~ 0.6
                  control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                  control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))
  
  ###Extract relevant information and store in the list
  model_summary <- model$summary.fixed
  bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model)
  model_DIC <- model$dic$dic
  model_WAIC <- model$waic$waic
  model_ppc <- model$cpo
  preds_model <- model$summary.fitted.values
  zhat <- exp(preds_model$mean) - 0.01
  eta_lower <- exp(preds_model$`0.025quant`) - 0.01
  eta_upper <- exp(preds_model$`0.975quant`) - 0.01
  preds_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 
                       zhat, eta_lower, eta_upper, preds_model)
  colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "cum_monthly_total_pm25_2",
                             "zhat", "eta_lower", "eta_upper","mean", "sd", 
                             "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")
  
  ###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
  prec_density = data.frame(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  
  # For each observation
  n_samples = 1000
  
  # For each observation
  for (i in 1:length(model$marginals.fitted.values)){
    sample_prec_i = inla.rmarginal(n_samples, inla.smarginal(prec_density))
    sample_sd_i = 1/sqrt(sample_prec_i)
    
    # Sample eta (linear predictor on log scale)
    row = data.frame(model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])
    sample_eta_i = inla.rmarginal(n_samples, inla.smarginal(row))
    
    sample_y = exp(rnorm(n_samples, sample_eta_i, sample_sd_i)) - 0.01
    sample_y[sample_y < 0] = 0
  
    ###Obtain 95% credible interval
    preds_model$y_ppmean[i] = mean(sample_y, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_pplower[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_ppupper[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  preds_model$interval_width = preds_model$`0.975quant` - preds_model$`0.025quant` 

  #Exponentiating parameter to get better interpretation of estimates 
  multeff <- exp(model$summary.fixed$mean)
  names(multeff) <- model$names.fixed
  
  #Plot of each parameters' posterior density 
  mf <- melt(model$marginals.fixed)
  cf <- spread(mf,Var2,value)
  names(cf)[2] <- 'parameter'
  param_plot = ggplot(cf,aes(x=x,y=y)) + geom_line()+facet_wrap(~ parameter, 
             scales="free") + geom_vline(xintercept=0) + ylab("density")
  
  #Plot of precision of random effect (main hyperparameter of interest)
  sden <- data.frame(bri.hyper.sd(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]]))
  hyperparam_plot = ggplot(sden,aes(x,y)) + geom_line() + ylab("density") + 
             xlab("linear predictor")
  
  #Store the results in the list
  kgr_model1_results = list(
    covmatrix = covGP1_treatment,
    prec = inv_covGP1_treatment,
    model_summary = model_summary,
    bri_hyperpar_summary = bri_hyperpar_summary,
    exp_effects = multeff,
    param_plot = param_plot,
    hyperparam_plot = hyperparam_plot,
    model_DIC = model_DIC,
    model_WAIC = model_WAIC,
    model_ppc = model_ppc,
    fitted_values = preds_model
  )
  
  return(kgr_model1_results)
}

#Fit kgr_model1
kgr_model1_treatment_fit = kgr_model1_treatment(dataset = inla_df_train)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model1_DIC = kgr_model1_treatment_fit$model_DIC
kgr_model1_WAIC = kgr_model1_treatment_fit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model1_treatment_fit$model_summary
kgr_model1_treatment_fit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model1_treatment_fit$exp_effects

#Show plots
kgr_model1_treatment_fit$param_plot
kgr_model1_treatment_fit$hyperparam_plot

for (i in 1:58){
  preds = kgr_model1_treatment_fit$fitted_values %>% filter(ID == i)

  # title = sprintf("Posterior Predictive Fits for County %s",i)
  title = sprintf("%s County",counties[i])

  post_pred_plot = preds %>% ggplot(aes(x=time,y=cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)) + geom_point() +
    geom_line(aes(y=zhat),color = "red") +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = eta_lower,ymax = eta_upper),
                fill = "orange",alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pplower,ymax = y_ppupper),fill = "skyblue",alpha = 0.3) + ggtitle(title) + theme_classic()
  print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

### Using K_time (AR6)

```{r,warning=FALSE}
kgr_model2_treatment = function(dataset,link=1){
  
  ###Fit INLA model 
  kgr_formula2 = (cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 + 0.01) ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + month + lead + co + so2 + no2 + o3 + pm10 + nonsmoke_pm25 + total_precip + avg_air_temp + avg_dew_point + avg_wind_speed + f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))
  
  #Switch to f(id2,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = inv_covGP2_treatment) for true K_time
  
  model = inla(formula = kgr_formula2,family = "lognormal",
               data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                  control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,
                                         return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                    control.family = list(hyper = list(prec = list(
                      prior = "gaussian",param = c(1, 0.5)))), #sigma ~ 0.6
                  control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                  control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))
  
  ###Extract relevant information and store in the list
  model_summary <- model$summary.fixed
  bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model)
  model_DIC <- model$dic$dic
  model_WAIC <- model$waic$waic
  model_ppc <- model$cpo
  preds_model <- model$summary.fitted.values
  zhat <- exp(preds_model$mean) - 0.01
  eta_lower <- exp(preds_model$`0.025quant`) - 0.01
  eta_upper <- exp(preds_model$`0.975quant`) - 0.01
  preds_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 
                       zhat, eta_lower, eta_upper, preds_model)
  colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "cum_monthly_total_pm25_2",
                             "zhat", "eta_lower", "eta_upper","mean", "sd", 
                             "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")
  
  ###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
  prec_density = data.frame(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  
  # For each observation
  n_samples = 1000
  
  # For each observation
  for (i in 1:length(model$marginals.fitted.values)){
    sample_prec_i = inla.rmarginal(n_samples, inla.smarginal(prec_density))
    sample_sd_i = 1/sqrt(sample_prec_i)
    
    # Sample eta (linear predictor on log scale)
    row = data.frame(model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])
    sample_eta_i = inla.rmarginal(n_samples, inla.smarginal(row))
    
    sample_y = exp(rnorm(n_samples, sample_eta_i, sample_sd_i)) - 0.01
    sample_y[sample_y < 0] = 0
  
    ###Obtain 95% credible interval
    preds_model$y_ppmean[i] = mean(sample_y, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_pplower[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_ppupper[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  preds_model$interval_width = preds_model$`0.975quant` - preds_model$`0.025quant` 

  #Exponentiating parameter to get better interpretation of estimates 
  multeff <- exp(model$summary.fixed$mean)
  names(multeff) <- model$names.fixed
  
  #Plot of each parameters' posterior density 
  mf <- melt(model$marginals.fixed)
  cf <- spread(mf,Var2,value)
  names(cf)[2] <- 'parameter'
  param_plot = ggplot(cf,aes(x=x,y=y)) + geom_line()+facet_wrap(~ parameter, 
             scales="free") + geom_vline(xintercept=0) + ylab("density")
  
  #Plot of precision of random effect (main hyperparameter of interest)
  sden <- data.frame(bri.hyper.sd(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]]))
  hyperparam_plot = ggplot(sden,aes(x,y)) + geom_line() + ylab("density") + 
             xlab("linear predictor")
  
  #Store the results in the list
  kgr_model2_results = list(
    model_summary = model_summary,
    bri_hyperpar_summary = bri_hyperpar_summary,
    exp_effects = multeff,
    param_plot = param_plot,
    hyperparam_plot = hyperparam_plot,
    model_DIC = model_DIC,
    model_WAIC = model_WAIC,
    model_ppc = model_ppc,
    fitted_values = preds_model
  )
  
  return(kgr_model2_results)
}

#Fit kgr_model2
kgr_model2_treatment_fit = kgr_model2_treatment(dataset = inla_df_train)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model2_DIC = kgr_model2_treatment_fit$model_DIC
kgr_model2_WAIC = kgr_model2_treatment_fit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$model_summary
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$exp_effects

#Show plots
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$param_plot
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$hyperparam_plot


for (i in 1:58){
  preds = kgr_model2_treatment_fit$fitted_values %>% filter(ID == i)

  # title = sprintf("Posterior Predictive Fits for County %s",i)
  title = sprintf("%s County",counties[i])

  post_pred_plot = preds %>% ggplot(aes(x=time,y=cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)) + geom_point() +
    geom_line(aes(y=zhat),color = "red") +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = eta_lower,ymax = eta_upper),
                fill = "orange",alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pplower,ymax = y_ppupper),fill = "skyblue",alpha = 0.3) + ggtitle(title) + theme_classic()
  print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

### Gamma model for comparison:

```{r,eval=FALSE}
kgr_model2_treatment_gamma = function(dataset,link=1){
  
  ###Fit INLA model 
  kgr_formula2 = (cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 + 0.0001) ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + month + lead + co + so2 + no2 + o3 + pm10 + nonsmoke_pm25 + total_precip + avg_air_temp + avg_dew_point + avg_wind_speed + f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))
  
  model = inla(formula = kgr_formula2,family = "gamma",data = dataset, num.threads = 20,
                  control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,
                                         return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                  control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                  control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = link))
  
  ###Extract relevant information and store in the list
  model_summary <- model$summary.fixed
  bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model)
  model_DIC <- model$dic$dic
  model_WAIC <- model$waic$waic
  preds_model <- model$summary.fitted.values
  preds_model <- cbind(dataset$NAME, dataset$time, dataset$ID, dataset$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, preds_model)
  colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "cum_monthly_total_pm25_2", "mean", "sd", 
                             "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")
  
  ###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
  for (i in 1:nrow(preds_model)){
    ###Sample 1000 mu for each row
    row = data.frame(model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])

    sample_mu = inla.rmarginal(1000,inla.smarginal(row))
    lower = quantile(sample_mu,0.025)
    mean = mean(sample_mu)
    upper = quantile(sample_mu,0.975)
    
    row$density = row$y / sum(row$y)
    
    phi_density = data.frame(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
    sample_phi = inla.rmarginal(1000,inla.smarginal(phi_density))
  

    ###Solve for a and b for each row
    a = sample_phi
    b = sample_phi / sample_mu #narrower
    # b = sample_mu / sample_phi #how INLA doc has it - leads to wider intervals

    ###Sample from Gamma with these a and b
    sample_y = rgamma(1000,a,b)
    
    ###Obtain 95% credible interval
    preds_model$y_ppmean[i] = mean(sample_y, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_pplower[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_ppupper[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  preds_model$interval_width = preds_model$`0.975quant` - preds_model$`0.025quant` 

  #Exponentiating parameter to get better interpretation of estimates 
  multeff <- exp(model$summary.fixed$mean)
  names(multeff) <- model$names.fixed
  
  #Plot of each parameters' posterior density 
  mf <- melt(model$marginals.fixed)
  cf <- spread(mf,Var2,value)
  names(cf)[2] <- 'parameter'
  param_plot = ggplot(cf,aes(x=x,y=y)) + geom_line()+facet_wrap(~ parameter, 
             scales="free") + geom_vline(xintercept=0) + ylab("density")
  
  #Plot of precision of random effect (main hyperparameter of interest)
  sden <- data.frame(bri.hyper.sd(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]]))
  hyperparam_plot = ggplot(sden,aes(x,y)) + geom_line() + ylab("density") + 
             xlab("linear predictor")
  
  #Store the results in the list
  kgr_model2_results = list(
    covmatrix = covGP2_treatment,
    prec = inv_covGP2_treatment,
    model_summary = model_summary,
    bri_hyperpar_summary = bri_hyperpar_summary,
    exp_effects = multeff,
    param_plot = param_plot,
    hyperparam_plot = hyperparam_plot,
    model_DIC = model_DIC,
    model_WAIC = model_WAIC,
    fitted_values = preds_model
  )
  
  return(kgr_model2_results)
}

#Fit kgr_model2
kgr_model2_treatment_fit_gamma = kgr_model2_treatment(dataset = inla_df_train)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model2_DIC = kgr_model2_treatment_fit$model_DIC
kgr_model2_WAIC = kgr_model2_treatment_fit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$model_summary
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$exp_effects

#Show plots
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$param_plot
kgr_model2_treatment_fit$hyperparam_plot


for (i in 1:58){
  df = inla_df %>% filter(ID == i) %>% dplyr::select(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)
  preds = kgr_model2_treatment_fit$fitted_values %>% filter(ID == i)
  df = cbind(df,preds)
  df$mean = df$mean - 0.0001

  # title = sprintf("Posterior Predictive Fits for County %s",i)
  title = sprintf("%s County",counties[i])

  post_pred_plot = preds %>% ggplot(aes(x=time,y=cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)) + geom_point() +
    geom_line(aes(y=mean),color = "red") + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = `0.025quant`,ymax = `0.975quant`), fill = "orange",alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pplower,ymax = y_ppupper),fill = "skyblue",alpha = 0.3) + ggtitle(title) + theme_classic()
  print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

# Calculate propensity scores -\> stabilized IPWs

## Function to extract density from fitted values

```{r}
est_density_ps = function(model_fit,model_preds){
  ps = rep(NA,nrow(model_preds))

  for (i in 1:nrow(model_preds)){
    row = data.frame(inla.smarginal(model_fit$marginals.fitted.values[[i]]))
    row$density = row$y / sum(row$y)

    obs_density = inla.dmarginal(model_preds$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[i],row) / sum(row$y)
    pred_density = inla.dmarginal(model_preds$mean[i],row) / sum(row$y)

    ps[i] = pred_density
  }

  return(ps)
}
# all_density_ps_denom = est_density_ps(all_ps_denom_model,preds_model)
```

## Estimate PS for all observations

We calculate stabilized propensity scores as: 

$SW_{i,t}(\bar Z, \bar X) = \prod_{k=0}^t \frac{f(Z_{i,k} | \bar Z_{i,k-1})}{f(Z_{i,k} | \bar Z_{i,k-1},\bar X_{i,k})}$

### Gamma (sw_prod)

```{r,cache=TRUE}
#Numerator model
ps_num_formula = (cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 + 0.01) ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + month + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag1 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag2 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag3 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag4 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag5 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag6 + f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))
  
all_ps_num_model = inla(ps_num_formula, family = "gamma",data = inla_df,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,
                                       return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

preds_model <- all_ps_num_model$summary.fitted.values
preds_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, preds_model)
colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "cum_monthly_total_pm25_2",
                           "mean", "sd","0.025quant", "0.5quant", 
                           "0.975quant", "mode")

all_density_ps_num = est_density_ps(all_ps_num_model,preds_model)

Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + month + percent_ili + percent_ili_lag1 + percent_ili_lag2 + percent_ili_lag3 + percent_ili_lag4 + percent_ili_lag5 + percent_ili_lag6 + lead + lead_lag1 + lead_lag2 + lead_lag3 + lead_lag4 + lead_lag5 + lead_lag6 + co + co_lag1 + co_lag2 + co_lag3 + co_lag4 + co_lag5 + co_lag6 + so2 + so2_lag1 + so2_lag2 + so2_lag3 + so2_lag4 + so2_lag5 + so2_lag6 + no2 + no2_lag1 + no2_lag2 + no2_lag3 + no2_lag4 + no2_lag5 + no2_lag6 + o3 + o3_lag1 + o3_lag2 + o3_lag3 + o3_lag4 + o3_lag5 + o3_lag6 + pm10 + pm10_lag1 + pm10_lag2 + pm10_lag3 + pm10_lag4 + pm10_lag5 + pm10_lag6 + nonsmoke_pm25 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag1 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag2 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag3 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag4 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag5 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag6 + total_precip + total_precip_lag1 + total_precip_lag2 + total_precip_lag3 + total_precip_lag4 + total_precip_lag5 + total_precip_lag6 + avg_air_temp + avg_air_temp_lag1 + avg_air_temp_lag2 + avg_air_temp_lag3 + avg_air_temp_lag4 + avg_air_temp_lag5 + avg_air_temp_lag6 + avg_dew_point + avg_dew_point_lag1 + avg_dew_point_lag2 + avg_dew_point_lag3 + avg_dew_point_lag4 + avg_dew_point_lag5 + avg_dew_point_lag6 + avg_wind_speed + avg_wind_speed_lag1 + avg_wind_speed_lag2 + avg_wind_speed_lag3 + avg_wind_speed_lag4 + avg_wind_speed_lag5 + avg_wind_speed_lag6 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag1 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag2 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag3 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag4 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag5 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag6 + f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))

all_ps_denom_model = inla(formula = ps_denom_formula,
                          family = "gamma",data = inla_df,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,
                                       return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.fixed = list(prec = 10),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

                           "cum_monthly_total_pm25_2","mean", "sd",
                           "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

denom_ps_allpreds = all_ps_denom_model$summary.fitted.values$mean

ev_sw_all_obs = num_ps_allpreds / denom_ps_allpreds

inla_df$sw1 = ev_sw_all_obs
inla_df$sw2 = density_sw_all_obs
```

```{r}
#Multiplying densities up to time k for each unit
inla_df$sw_prod = 0

for (i in counties){
  county_idx = which(inla_df$NAME == i)
  county_sw = inla_df$sw2[county_idx]
  sw_prod = county_sw[1]
  
  for(j in 2:72){
    sw_prod[j] = county_sw[j]*sw_prod[j-1]
  }
  
  inla_df$sw_prod[county_idx] = sw_prod
}

summary(inla_df$sw_prod)
```

### Log normal (sw_prod2)

```{r}
#LOGNORMAL
est_density_ps2 = function(model_fit,model_preds){
 ps = rep(NA,nrow(model_preds))
 for (i in 1:nrow(model_preds)){
   row = data.frame(inla.smarginal(model_fit$marginals.fitted.values[[i]]))
   
   row$x = exp(row$x)
   row$density = row$y / sum(row$y)
   
   obs_density = inla.dmarginal(model_preds$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[i],row) / sum(row$y)
   pred_density = inla.dmarginal(model_preds$zhat[i],row) / sum(row$y)
   
   ps[i] = pred_density
 }
 return(ps)
}
```

```{r,cache=TRUE}
#Numerator model
all_ps_num_model2 = inla(formula = ps_num_formula,
                          family = "lognormal",data = inla_df,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,
                                       return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.family = list(hyper = list(prec = list(
                    prior = "gaussian",param = c(1, 0.5)))), #sigma ~ 0.6
                control.inla = list(strategy = "laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

preds_model <- all_ps_num_model2$summary.fitted.values
zhat <- exp(preds_model$mean) - 0.01
eta_lower <- exp(preds_model$`0.025quant`) - 0.01
eta_upper <- exp(preds_model$`0.975quant`) - 0.01
preds_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, zhat, eta_lower, eta_upper, preds_model)
colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "cum_monthly_total_pm25_2",
                           "zhat", "eta_lower", "eta_upper","mean", "sd", 
                           "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

all_density_ps_num2 = est_density_ps2(all_ps_num_model2,preds_model)

                control.family = list(hyper = list(prec = list(

                control.inla = list(strategy = "laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

                           "zhat", "eta_lower", "eta_upper","mean", "sd",
```

```{r}
inla_df$sw_prod2 = 0

for (i in counties){
  county_idx = which(inla_df$NAME == i)
  county_sw = density_sw_all_obs2[county_idx]
  sw_prod = county_sw[1]
```

### Gamma model PPCs

```{r}
wildfire_months = c(1,2,7:12)
wildfire_idx = inla_df$month %in% wildfire_months
```

#### CPO

$CPO_i = p(y_i \mid \bf{y}_{-i}) \approx \frac{1}{E_{\theta \mid \bf{y}}[1/p(y_i / \theta)]}$

```{r}
cpo_vals = all_ps_denom_model$cpo$cpo

mean(cpo_vals)

# Identify problematic observations
sum(all_ps_denom_model$cpo$failure > 0) #1888 failures

cpo_outliers <- which(cpo_vals < 0.01)

length(cpo_outliers) / length(cpo_vals)

ps_ppc_df = inla_df %>% select(ID,NAME,date,time,month,deaths,cum_monthly_total_pm25_2) %>% cbind(cpo_vals)

spatiotemporal_cpo = ps_ppc_df %>% group_by(NAME,month) %>% summarize(bad_preds = length(which(cpo_vals <= 0.01)))

unique(spatiotemporal_cpo$NAME[which(spatiotemporal_cpo$bad_preds == 6)])

for (i in 1:58){
title = sprintf("CPO values for %s County",counties[i])

post_pred_plot = ps_ppc_df %>% filter(ID == i) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=time,y=cpo_vals)) + geom_point() + 
  geom_line(aes(y=0.01),color = "red") + ggtitle(title) + 
  theme_classic()

print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

Seems like model has trouble predicting months 7-11 aka peak wildfire season. Naturally so since these are where outliers will occur. Number of bad preds in these months range from 3-6. The counties with 6 outliers for a month are basically Sacramento or north... maybe need stronger spatial dependence for those regions?

```{r}
#CPO analysis by wildfire szn
wf_cpo_vals = cpo_vals[wildfire_idx]
nonwf_cpo_vals = cpo_vals[!wildfire_idx]

nonwf_cpo_outliers <- which(nonwf_cpo_vals < 0.01)
length(nonwf_cpo_outliers) / length(nonwf_cpo_vals)

wf_cpo_outliers <- which(wf_cpo_vals < 0.01)
length(wf_cpo_outliers) / length(wf_cpo_vals)
```

#### PIT

$PIT_i = P(Y_i^{rep} \leq y_i \mid \bf{y}_{-i}) = \int_{-\infty}^{y_i} p(y \mid \bf{y}_i) dy$

```{r}
pit_vals = all_ps_denom_model$cpo$pit

hist(pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line


df_pit <- data.frame(pit = pit_vals)

gamma_pit_plot <- ggplot(df_pit, aes(x = pit)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    bins = 50,
    boundary = 0,
    closed = "left"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Gamma PIT Histogram",
    x = "PIT",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

gamma_pit_plot



# Formal uniformity test
ks.test(pit_vals, "punif")  # Kolmogorov-Smirnov test

ps_ppc_df = cbind(ps_ppc_df,pit_vals)

spatiotemporal_pit = ps_ppc_df %>% group_by(NAME,month) %>% summarize(bad_preds = length(which(pit_vals > 0.95)))

for (i in 1:58){
title = sprintf("PIT values for %s County",counties[i])

post_pred_plot = ps_ppc_df %>% filter(ID == i) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=time,y=pit_vals)) + geom_point() + 
  geom_line(aes(y=1),color = "red") + ggtitle(title) + 
  theme_classic()

print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

```{r}
#PIT analysis by wildfire szn
wf_pit_vals = pit_vals[wildfire_idx]
nonwf_pit_vals = pit_vals[!wildfire_idx]

hist(wf_pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line

hist(nonwf_pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line
```

#### RMSE + MAR

```{r,cache=TRUE}
#ORIGINAL
n_samples = 100 

obs_z = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2
resids = obs_z - all_ps_denom_model$summary.fitted.values$mean
RMSE = sqrt(mean(resids^2))
MAR = max(abs(resids))

pp_rmse = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mar = numeric(n_samples)

# For each posterior sample
for (j in 1:n_samples){
  y_rep = numeric(length(all_ps_denom_model$marginals.fitted.values))
  
  # Sample hyperparameter (phi) once per replicate dataset
  phi_density = data.frame(all_ps_denom_model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  sample_phi = inla.rmarginal(1, inla.smarginal(phi_density))
  
  # For each observation
  for (i in 1:length(all_ps_denom_model$marginals.fitted.values)){
    # Sample fitted value (mu) from its marginal
    row = data.frame(all_ps_denom_model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])
    sample_mu = inla.rmarginal(1, inla.smarginal(row))
    
    # Generate replicate observation from Gamma(shape=phi, rate=phi/mu)
    a = sample_phi
    b = sample_phi / sample_mu
    y_rep[i] = rgamma(1, shape=a, rate=b)
  }
  
  # Compute test statistics for this replicate
  # Use the SAME fitted values used for observed residuals
  rep_resids = y_rep - all_ps_denom_model$summary.fitted.values$mean
  pp_rmse[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids^2))
  pp_mar[j] = max(abs(rep_resids))
}

# Bayesian p-values
p_rmse = mean(pp_rmse >= RMSE)
p_mar = mean(pp_mar >= MAR)

cat("Observed RMSE:", RMSE, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated RMSE:", mean(pp_rmse), "\n")
cat("P-value RMSE:", p_rmse, "\n\n")

cat("Observed MAR:", MAR, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAR:", mean(pp_mar), "\n")
cat("P-value MAR:", p_mar, "\n")

# Visualize
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
hist(pp_rmse, main="Posterior Predictive RMSE", xlab="RMSE")
abline(v=RMSE, col="red", lwd=2)

hist(pp_mar, main="Posterior Predictive MAR", xlab="MAR")
abline(v=MAR, col="red", lwd=2)
```

```{r,eval = TRUE}
#SPLIT BY WILDFIRE SZN
n_samples = 100

obs_z = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2
resids = obs_z - preds_model$zhat
#resids = obs_z - all_ps_denom_model2$summary.fitted.values$mean

# Subset statistics
RMSE_wf = sqrt(mean(resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
RMSE_nonwf = sqrt(mean(resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))

MAR_wf = max(abs(resids[wildfire_idx]))
MAR_nonwf = max(abs(resids[!wildfire_idx]))

pp_rmse_wf = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mar_wf = numeric(n_samples)

pp_rmse_nonwf = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mar_nonwf = numeric(n_samples)

# For each posterior sample
for (j in 1:n_samples){
  y_rep = numeric(length(all_ps_denom_model$marginals.fitted.values))
  
  # Sample hyperparameter (phi) once per replicate dataset
  phi_density = data.frame(all_ps_denom_model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  sample_phi = inla.rmarginal(1, inla.smarginal(phi_density))
  
  # For each observation
  for (i in 1:length(all_ps_denom_model$marginals.fitted.values)){
    # Sample fitted value (mu) from its marginal
    row = data.frame(all_ps_denom_model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])
    sample_mu = inla.rmarginal(1, inla.smarginal(row))
    
    # Generate replicate observation from Gamma(shape=phi, rate=phi/mu)
    a = sample_phi
    b = sample_phi / sample_mu
    y_rep[i] = rgamma(1, shape=a, rate=b)
  }
  
  # Compute test statistics for this replicate
  # Use the SAME fitted values used for observed residuals
  rep_resids = y_rep - all_ps_denom_model$summary.fitted.values$mean

  pp_rmse_wf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
  pp_mar_wf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]))

  pp_rmse_nonwf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))
  pp_mar_nonwf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]))  
}

# Bayesian p-values
p_rmse_wf = mean(pp_rmse_wf >= RMSE_wf)
p_mar_wf = mean(pp_mar_wf >= MAR_wf)

p_rmse_nonwf = mean(pp_rmse_nonwf >= RMSE_nonwf)
p_mar_nonwf = mean(pp_mar_nonwf >= MAR_nonwf)

cat('Replicates:',pp_rmse_wf, 'vs. observed:',RMSE_wf)
cat('Replicates:',pp_mar_wf, 'vs. observed:',MAR_wf)

cat('Replicates:',pp_rmse_nonwf, 'vs. observed:',RMSE_nonwf)
cat('Replicates:',pp_mar_nonwf, 'vs. observed:',MAR_nonwf)

# Visualize
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
hist(pp_rmse_nonwf, main="Posterior Predictive RMSE", xlab="RMSE")
abline(v=RMSE_nonwf, col="red", lwd=2)

hist(pp_mar_nonwf, main="Posterior Predictive MAR", xlab="MAR")
abline(v=MAR_nonwf, col="red", lwd=2)
```

### Log Normal PPCs

#### CPO

$CPO_i = p(y_i \mid \bf{y}_{-i}) \approx \frac{1}{E_{\theta \mid \bf{y}}[1/p(y_i / \theta)]}$

```{r}
cpo_vals = all_ps_denom_model2$cpo$cpo
mean(cpo_vals)

# Identify problematic observations
sum(all_ps_denom_model2$cpo$failure > 0) #no failures

cpo_outliers <- which(cpo_vals < 0.01)

length(cpo_outliers) / length(cpo_vals)

ps_ppc_df = inla_df %>% select(ID,NAME,date,time,month,deaths,cum_monthly_total_pm25_2) %>% cbind(cpo_vals)

spatiotemporal_cpo = ps_ppc_df %>% group_by(NAME,month) %>% summarize(bad_preds = length(which(cpo_vals <= 0.01)))

unique(spatiotemporal_cpo$NAME[which(spatiotemporal_cpo$bad_preds == 6)])

for (i in 1:58){
title = sprintf("CPO values for %s County",counties[i])

post_pred_plot = ps_ppc_df %>% filter(ID == i) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=time,y=cpo_vals)) + geom_point() + 
  geom_line(aes(y=0.01),color = "red") + ggtitle(title) + 
  theme_classic()

print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

```{r}
#CPO analysis by wildfire szn
wf_cpo_vals = cpo_vals[wildfire_idx]
nonwf_cpo_vals = cpo_vals[!wildfire_idx]

nonwf_cpo_outliers <- which(nonwf_cpo_vals < 0.01)
length(nonwf_cpo_outliers) / length(nonwf_cpo_vals)

wf_cpo_outliers <- which(wf_cpo_vals < 0.01)
length(wf_cpo_outliers) / length(wf_cpo_vals)
```

#### PIT

$PIT_i = P(Y_i^{rep} \leq y_i \mid \bf{y}_{-i}) = \int_{-\infty}^{y_i} p(y \mid \bf{y}_i) dy$

```{r}
pit_vals = all_ps_denom_model2$cpo$pit

hist(pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line


df_pit <- data.frame(pit = pit_vals)


# GGplot PIT histogram
lognormal_pit_plot <- ggplot(df_pit, aes(x = pit)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    bins = 50,
    boundary = 0,
    closed = "left"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Log Normal PIT Histogram",
    x = "PIT",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


lognormal_pit_plot

# Formal uniformity test
ks.test(pit_vals, "punif")  # Kolmogorov-Smirnov test

ps_ppc_df = cbind(ps_ppc_df,pit_vals)

spatiotemporal_pit = ps_ppc_df %>% group_by(NAME,month) %>% summarize(bad_preds = length(which(pit_vals > 0.95)))

for (i in 1:58){
title = sprintf("PIT values for %s County",counties[i])

post_pred_plot = ps_ppc_df %>% filter(ID == i) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=time,y=pit_vals)) + geom_point() + 
  geom_line(aes(y=1),color = "red") + ggtitle(title) + 
  theme_classic()

print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

```{r}
exposure_pit_panel <- gamma_pit_plot + lognormal_pit_plot

#   width = 12,

#   dpi = 600

```

```{r}
nonwf_pit_vals = pit_vals[!wildfire_idx]

hist(wf_pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line

hist(nonwf_pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line
```

```{r,cache=TRUE}
n_samples = 10

obs_z = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2
resids = log(obs_z + 1e-3) - all_ps_denom_model2$summary.fitted.values$mean
# resids = obs_z - exp(all_ps_denom_model2$summary.fitted.values$mean)
RMSE = sqrt(mean(resids^2))
MAR = max(abs(resids))
MAD = median(abs(resids))

obs_county_metrics = matrix(NA, nrow = 58, ncol = 3)
colnames(obs_county_metrics) = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")

for (k in 1:58){
  start = (k-1)*72 + 1
  end = k*72
  county_resids = resids[start:end]

  obs_county_metrics[k,1] = sqrt(mean(county_resids^2))
  obs_county_metrics[k,2] = max(abs(county_resids))
  obs_county_metrics[k,3] = median(abs(county_resids))
}

obs_wildfire_szn_metrics = matrix(NA, nrow = 2, ncol = 3)
colnames(obs_wildfire_szn_metrics) = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")

wildfire_szn_resids = resids[wildfire_idx]
nonwildfire_szn_resids = resids[!wildfire_idx]

obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[1,1] = sqrt(mean(wildfire_szn_resids^2))
obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[1,2] = max(abs(wildfire_szn_resids))
obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[1,3] = median(abs(wildfire_szn_resids))

obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[2,1] = sqrt(mean(nonwildfire_szn_resids^2))
obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[2,2] = max(abs(nonwildfire_szn_resids))
obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[2,3] = median(abs(nonwildfire_szn_resids))

rep_county_metrics = array(
  NA,
  dim = c(n_samples, 58, 3),
  dimnames = list(
    sample = 1:n_samples,
    county = 1:58,
    metric = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")
  )
)

rep_wildfire_szn_metrics = array(
  NA,
  dim = c(n_samples, 2, 3),
  dimnames = list(
    sample = 1:n_samples,
    wildfire_szn = c(1,0),
    metric = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")
  )
)

pp_rmse = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mar = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mad = numeric(n_samples)

# For each posterior sample
for (j in 1:n_samples){
  y_rep = numeric(length(all_ps_denom_model2$marginals.fitted.values))
  
  # Sample hyperparameter (tau) once per replicate dataset
  prec_density = data.frame(all_ps_denom_model2$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  sample_prec = inla.rmarginal(1, inla.smarginal(prec_density))
  sample_sd = 1/sqrt(sample_prec)
  
  # For each observation
  for (i in 1:length(all_ps_denom_model2$marginals.fitted.values)){
    row = data.frame(all_ps_denom_model2$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])
    sample_eta = inla.rmarginal(1, inla.smarginal(row))
    
    y_rep[i] = exp(rnorm(1,sample_eta,sample_sd))
  }
  
  # Compute test statistics for this replicate
  # Use the SAME fitted values used for observed residuals
  rep_resids = log(y_rep + 1e-3) - all_ps_denom_model2$summary.fitted.values$mean
  
  pp_rmse[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids^2))
  pp_mar[j] = max(abs(rep_resids))
  pp_mad[j] = median(abs(rep_resids))
  
  #Stratify results by county 
  for (k in 1:58){
    
    start = (k-1)*72 + 1
    end = k*72
    county_resids = rep_resids[start:end]
    
    rep_county_metrics[j, k, "RMSE"] = sqrt(mean(county_resids^2))
    rep_county_metrics[j, k, "MAR"]  = max(abs(county_resids))
    rep_county_metrics[j, k, "MAD"]  = median(abs(county_resids))
  }
  
  #Stratify results by wildfire szn
  wildfire_szn_resids = rep_resids[wildfire_idx]
  nonwildfire_szn_resids = rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]
  
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 1, "RMSE"] = sqrt(mean(wildfire_szn_resids^2))
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 1, "MAR"]  = max(abs(wildfire_szn_resids))
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 1, "MAD"]  = median(abs(wildfire_szn_resids))
  
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 2, "RMSE"] = sqrt(mean(nonwildfire_szn_resids^2))
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 2, "MAR"]  = max(abs(nonwildfire_szn_resids))
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 2, "MAD"]  = median(abs(nonwildfire_szn_resids))

  # pp_rmse_wf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
  # pp_mar_wf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]))
  # 
  # pp_rmse_nonwf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))
  # pp_mar_nonwf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]))  
}

# Bayesian p-values
p_rmse = mean(pp_rmse >= RMSE)
p_mar = mean(pp_mar >= MAR)
p_mad = mean(pp_mad >= MAD)

cat("Observed RMSE:", RMSE, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated RMSE:", mean(pp_rmse), "\n")
cat("P-value RMSE:", p_rmse, "\n\n")

cat("Observed MAR:", MAR, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAR:", mean(pp_mar), "\n")
cat("P-value MAR:", p_mar, "\n")

cat("Observed MAD:", MAD, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAD:", mean(pp_mad), "\n")
cat("P-value MAD:", p_mad, "\n")
```

Stratifying by county:

```{r}
plot_df <- data.frame(
  value = as.vector(rep_county_metrics),
  replicate = rep(1:n_samples, times = 58 * 3),
  county = rep(rep(counties, each = n_samples), times = 3),
  metric = rep(c("RMSE", "MAR", "MAD"), each = n_samples * 58)
)

plot_df$county = factor(plot_df$county)

county_pops = inla_df %>% group_by(NAME) %>% summarize(avg_pop = mean(Total_Pop))

plot_df$log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop)

# Reorder county factor levels by population size
plot_df$county = factor(plot_df$county, levels = counties[order(county_pops$avg_pop)])
plot_df$log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop[match(plot_df$county, counties)])

observed_df = data.frame(
  log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop),
  observed_rmse = obs_county_metrics[,"RMSE"],
  observed_mar = obs_county_metrics[,"MAR"],
  observed_mad = obs_county_metrics[,"MAD"],
  county = factor(counties, levels = counties[order(county_pops$avg_pop)])
)

#RMSE
rmse_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "RMSE") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = county, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = county, y = observed_rmse), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "County", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive RMSE",
    title = "Posterior Predictive RMSE Distributions by County"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) 

# ggsave(rmse_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Exposure-RMSE-PPC-byCounty.png",dpi = 600)

rmse_plot

#Median absolute deviation
mad_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "MAD") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = county, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = county, y = observed_mad), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "County", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive MAD",
    title = "Posterior Predictive MAD Distributions by County"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# ggsave(mad_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Exposure-MAD-PPC-byCounty.png",dpi = 600)

mad_plot
```
