## Checking numerator GPS can be derived from denominator GPS

$f(Z_{i,k} | \bar Z_{i,k-1}) = \int f(Z_{i,k} | \bar Z_{i,k-1},\bar X_{i,k}) f(\bar X_{i,k} | \bar Z_{i,k-1}) dX \approx \sum f(Z_{i,k} | \bar Z_{i,k-1},\bar X_{i,k}) * \frac{1}{n}$

How to check?

1.  Identify a treatment history to condition on
2.  Identify observations in the data with that treatment history
3.  Extract the predicted propensity scores from numerator and denominator model for those observations
4.  Average them over observed covariates and compare the averages (should be similar)

```{r}
#mean(all_density_ps_denom) - mean(all_density_ps_num)

threshold = 5

subset_idx <- which(
        abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag1) < threshold &
        abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag2) < threshold &
        abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag3) < threshold &
        abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag4) < threshold &
        abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag5) < threshold &
        abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag6) < threshold)

cat("Number of observations with low treatment for all 6 lags:", length(subset_idx), "\n")

# subset_idx = which(abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag1) < threshold &
#        abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag2) < threshold &
#        abs(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag3) < threshold)
# 
# cat("Number of observations with low treatment for all 3 lags:", length(subset_idx), "\n")

subset_num_densities = all_density_ps_num2[subset_idx]
subset_denom_densities = all_density_ps_denom2[subset_idx]

mean_numer_density = mean(subset_num_densities)
mean_denom_density = mean(subset_denom_densities)


cat("=== MARGINALIZATION CHECK ===\n")
cat("Mean density from DENOMINATOR model (conditional on X̄):", mean_denom_density, "\n")
cat("Mean density from NUMERATOR model (marginal):", mean_numer_density, "\n")
cat("Relative difference:", 
    round(100 * abs(mean_denom_density - mean_numer_density) / mean_numer_density, 2), "%\n")
cat("Ratio (denom/numer, should be ≈ 1):", 
    round(mean_denom_density / mean_numer_density, 3), "\n\n")
```

Given that for different values of threshold, the largest difference is only about 10%, I think the two model approach gives results reasonably consistent with marginalization

## Checking covariate balancing property

```{r,cache=TRUE,eval=FALSE}
###Without GPS
summary(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)

group1 = which(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 <= 5) #3013
group2 = which(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 > 5 & inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 <= 50) #1032
group3 = which(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 > 50) #1042

# mu1 = mean(inla_df$avg_air_temp[group1],na.rm = TRUE)
# mu2 = mean(inla_df$avg_air_temp[-group1],na.rm = TRUE)

t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[group3]), y = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[-group3]))


###GPS adjusted 

#median treatment = 5.0217 -> set Z = 5 for all observations and recalculate SWs
inla_df62 = inla_df
inla_df62$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 = 62

ps_num_formula = (cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 + 0.1) ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + month + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag1 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag2 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag3 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag4 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag5 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag6 + f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))


all_ps_num_model62 = inla(formula = ps_num_formula,family = "lognormal",
                          data = inla_df62,num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,
                                       return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.family = list(hyper = list(prec = list(
                    prior = "gaussian",param = c(1, 0.5)))), #sigma ~ 0.6
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

preds_model62 <- all_ps_num_model62$summary.fitted.values
zhat <- exp(preds_model62$mean) - 0.01
eta_lower <- exp(preds_model62$`0.025quant`) - 0.01
eta_upper <- exp(preds_model62$`0.975quant`) - 0.01
preds_model62 <- cbind(inla_df62$NAME, inla_df62$time, inla_df62$ID, inla_df62$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 
                       zhat, eta_lower, eta_upper, preds_model62)
colnames(preds_model62) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "cum_monthly_total_pm25_2",
                           "zhat", "eta_lower", "eta_upper","mean", "sd", 
                           "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

all_density_ps_num62 = est_density_ps(all_ps_num_model62,preds_model62)


ps_denom_formula = (cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 + 0.1) ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + month + percent_ili + percent_ili_lag1 + percent_ili_lag2 + percent_ili_lag3 + percent_ili_lag4 + percent_ili_lag5 + percent_ili_lag6 + lead + lead_lag1 + lead_lag2 + lead_lag3 + lead_lag4 + lead_lag5 + lead_lag6 + co + co_lag1 + co_lag2 + co_lag3 + co_lag4 + co_lag5 + co_lag6 + so2 + so2_lag1 + so2_lag2 + so2_lag3 + so2_lag4 + so2_lag5 + so2_lag6 + no2 + no2_lag1 + no2_lag2 + no2_lag3 + no2_lag4 + no2_lag5 + no2_lag6 + o3 + o3_lag1 + o3_lag2 + o3_lag3 + o3_lag4 + o3_lag5 + o3_lag6 + pm10 + pm10_lag1 + pm10_lag2 + pm10_lag3 + pm10_lag4 + pm10_lag5 + pm10_lag6 + nonsmoke_pm25 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag1 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag2 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag3 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag4 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag5 + nonsmoke_pm25_lag6 + total_precip + total_precip_lag1 + total_precip_lag2 + total_precip_lag3 + total_precip_lag4 + total_precip_lag5 + total_precip_lag6 + avg_air_temp + avg_air_temp_lag1 + avg_air_temp_lag2 + avg_air_temp_lag3 + avg_air_temp_lag4 + avg_air_temp_lag5 + avg_air_temp_lag6 + avg_dew_point + avg_dew_point_lag1 + avg_dew_point_lag2 + avg_dew_point_lag3 + avg_dew_point_lag4 + avg_dew_point_lag5 + avg_dew_point_lag6 + avg_wind_speed + avg_wind_speed_lag1 + avg_wind_speed_lag2 + avg_wind_speed_lag3 + avg_wind_speed_lag4 + avg_wind_speed_lag5 + avg_wind_speed_lag6 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag1 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag2 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag3 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag4 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag5 + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_lag6 + f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))

all_ps_denom_model62 = inla(formula = ps_denom_formula,family = "lognormal",
                            data = inla_df62,num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,
                                       return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.family = list(hyper = list(prec = list(
                    prior = "gaussian",param = c(1, 0.5)))), #sigma ~ 0.6
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

preds_model62 <- all_ps_denom_model62$summary.fitted.values
zhat <- exp(preds_model62$mean) - 0.01
eta_lower <- exp(preds_model62$`0.025quant`) - 0.01
eta_upper <- exp(preds_model62$`0.975quant`) - 0.01
preds_model62 <- cbind(inla_df62$NAME, inla_df62$time, inla_df62$ID, inla_df62$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, 
                       zhat, eta_lower, eta_upper, preds_model62)
colnames(preds_model62) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "cum_monthly_total_pm25_2",
                           "zhat", "eta_lower", "eta_upper","mean", "sd", 
                           "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

all_density_ps_denom62 = est_density_ps(all_ps_denom_model62,preds_model62)

density_sw_all_obs62 = all_density_ps_num62 / all_density_ps_denom62

# summary(all_density_ps_num5)
# summary(all_density_ps_denom5)
summary(density_sw_all_obs62)

inla_df62$sw = density_sw_all_obs62

#Multiplying densities up to time k for each unit
inla_df62$sw_prod = 0

for (i in counties){
  county_idx = which(inla_df62$NAME == i)
  county_sw = inla_df62$sw[county_idx]
  sw_prod = county_sw[1]
  
  for(j in 2:72){
    sw_prod[j] = county_sw[j]*sw_prod[j-1]
  }
  
  inla_df62$sw_prod[county_idx] = sw_prod
}

summary(inla_df62$sw_prod)
```

```{r,eval=FALSE}
#Split GPS values into 4 groups
gps_group1 = which(inla_df62$sw_prod <= 1e-4) #395
gps_group2 = which(inla_df62$sw_prod > 1e-4 & inla_df62$sw_prod <= 1e-3) #60
gps_group3 = which(inla_df62$sw_prod > 1e-3 & inla_df62$sw_prod <= 1e-2) #60
gps_group4 = which(inla_df62$sw_prod > 0.01) #107

# summary(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[gps_group1])

#For each GPS group, compare avg X between original treatment groups 
nested_group1 = which(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[gps_group1] <= 5) #1875 / 3949
nested_group2 = which(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[gps_group2] <= 5) #60 / 60
nested_group3 = which(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[gps_group3] <= 5) #60 /60
nested_group4 = which(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[gps_group4] <= 5) #107 / 107

#T-test for each covariate
test1 = t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[nested_group1]), y = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[-nested_group1]))
test2 = t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[nested_group2]), y = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[-nested_group2]))
test3 = t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[nested_group3]), y = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[-nested_group3]))
test4 = t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[nested_group4]), y = na.omit(inla_df$avg_dew_point[-nested_group4]))


d1 = test1$estimate[1] - test1$estimate[2]
d2 = test2$estimate[1] - test2$estimate[2]
d3 = test3$estimate[1] - test3$estimate[2]
d4 = test4$estimate[1] - test4$estimate[2]

se1 = test1$stderr
se2 = test2$stderr
se3 = test3$stderr
se4 = test4$stderr

n1 = length(nested_group1) #nested_group size
n2 = length(nested_group2)
n3 = length(nested_group3)
n4 = length(nested_group4)

num = n1*d1 + n2*d2 + n3*d3 + n4*d4
denom = sqrt(n1^2*se1^2 + n2^2*se2^2 + n3^2*se3^2 + n4^2*se4^2) 

weighted_mean_diff = num / (n1+n2+n3+n4)
se_diff = denom / (n1+n2+n3+n4)

tstat = num/denom 
```

```{r,eval=FALSE}
#Automate for all covariates -> make a table
covariate_balancing_df = matrix(NA,nrow = 13,ncol=3)
tracker = 1

for (i in 10:22){
  test1 = t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df[nested_group1,i]), y = na.omit(inla_df[-nested_group1,i]))
  test2 = t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df[nested_group2,i]), y = na.omit(inla_df[-nested_group2,i]))
  test3 = t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df[nested_group3,i]), y = na.omit(inla_df[-nested_group3,i]))
  test4 = t.test(x = na.omit(inla_df[nested_group4,i]), y = na.omit(inla_df[-nested_group4,i]))
  
  
  d1 = test1$estimate[1] - test1$estimate[2]
  d2 = test2$estimate[1] - test2$estimate[2]
  d3 = test3$estimate[1] - test3$estimate[2]
  d4 = test4$estimate[1] - test4$estimate[2]
  
  se1 = test1$stderr
  se2 = test2$stderr
  se3 = test3$stderr
  se4 = test4$stderr
  
  n1 = length(nested_group1) #nested_group size
  n2 = length(nested_group2)
  n3 = length(nested_group3)
  n4 = length(nested_group4)
  
  num = n1*d1 + n2*d2 + n3*d3 + n4*d4
  denom = sqrt(n1^2*se1^2 + n2^2*se2^2 + n3^2*se3^2 + n4^2*se4^2) 
  
  weighted_mean_diff = num / (n1+n2+n3+n4)
  se_diff = denom / (n1+n2+n3+n4)
  
  tstat = num/denom 
  
  covariate_balancing_df[tracker,] = c(weighted_mean_diff,se_diff,tstat)
  tracker = tracker+1
}

covariate_balancing_df = as.data.frame(covariate_balancing_df)
covariate_balancing_df = cbind(colnames(inla_df)[10:22],covariate_balancing_df)
colnames(covariate_balancing_df) = c("covariate","weighted_diff","se","t-statistic")
covariate_balancing_df = covariate_balancing_df[-9,]
```

# Modeling mortality

## KGR models

### Using K_cov

```{r}
kgr_model1_response_nb = function(dataset,link=1){
  
  ###Fit INLA model 
  kgr_formula1 = deaths ~ -1 + offset(log(Total_Pop)) + month + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2*Cluster + pm25 + total_precip + avg_air_temp + avg_dew_point + avg_wind_speed + f(id2,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = inv_covGP1_treatment)
  
  model = inla(formula = kgr_formula1,family = "nbinomial",data = dataset, num.threads = 20,
                  control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE, config = TRUE,
                                         return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                  #control.family = list(variant = 1), E = Total_Pop,
                  control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                  control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                  control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = link))
  
  ###Extract relevant information and store in the list
  model_summary <- model$summary.fixed
  bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model)
  model_DIC <- model$dic$dic
  model_WAIC <- model$waic$waic
  preds_model <- model$summary.fitted.values
  preds_model <- cbind(dataset$NAME, dataset$time, dataset$ID, dataset$deaths, preds_model)
  colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

  pp_ysamples = list()
  
  ###NBINOMIAL SAMPLING
  
  n_density = data.frame(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  sample_n = inla.rmarginal(1000,inla.smarginal(n_density))
  pp_nsamples = sample_n  
  
  ###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
  for (i in 1:nrow(preds_model)){
    ###Sample 1000 mu for each row
    row = data.frame(model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])

    sample_mu = inla.rmarginal(1000,inla.smarginal(row))
    lower = quantile(sample_mu,0.025)
    mean = mean(sample_mu)
    upper = quantile(sample_mu,0.975)
    
    ###Sample from nbinom with these a and b
    sample_y = rnbinom(n = 1000, size = sample_n, mu = sample_mu)
    pp_ysamples[[i]] = sample_y
    
    ###Obtain 95% credible interval
    preds_model$y_ppmean[i] = mean(sample_y, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_pplower[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_ppupper[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  # preds_model$interval_width = preds_model$`0.975quant` - preds_model$`0.025quant` 
  # marginal_fvs <- model$marginals.fitted.values

  # #Exponentiating parameter to get better interpretation of estimates 
  # multeff <- exp(model$summary.fixed$mean)
  # names(multeff) <- model$names.fixed
  # 
  # #Plot of each parameters' posterior density 
  # mf <- melt(model$marginals.fixed)
  # cf <- spread(mf,Var2,value)
  # names(cf)[2] <- 'parameter'
  # param_plot = ggplot(cf,aes(x=x,y=y)) + geom_line()+facet_wrap(~ parameter, 
  #            scales="free") + geom_vline(xintercept=0) + ylab("density")
  
  #Plot of precision of random effect (main hyperparameter of interest)
  # sden <- data.frame(bri.hyper.sd(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]]))
  # hyperparam_plot = ggplot(sden,aes(x,y)) + geom_line() + ylab("density") + 
  #            xlab("linear predictor")
  
  #Store the results in the list
  kgr_model1_results = list(
    covmatrix = covGP1_response,
    prec = inv_covGP1_response,
    model_summary = model_summary,
    bri_hyperpar_summary = bri_hyperpar_summary,
    #exp_effects = multeff,
    #param_plot = param_plot,
    #hyperparam_plot = hyperparam_plot,
    model_DIC = model_DIC,
    model_WAIC = model_WAIC,
    fitted_values = preds_model,
    pp_ysamples = pp_ysamples,
    pp_nsamples = pp_nsamples
  )
  
  return(kgr_model1_results)
}

#Fit kgr_model1
kgr_model1_response_fit = kgr_model1_response_nb(dataset = inla_df_train)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model1_DIC = kgr_model1_response_fit$model_DIC
kgr_model1_WAIC = kgr_model1_response_fit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model1_response_fit$model_summary
kgr_model1_response_fit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model1_response_fit$exp_effects

#Show plots
kgr_model1_response_fit$param_plot
kgr_model1_response_fit$hyperparam_plot

for (i in 1:58){
  df = inla_df_train %>% filter(ID == i) %>% dplyr::select(deaths)
  preds = kgr_model1_response_fit$fitted_values %>% filter(ID == i)
  df = cbind(df,preds)
  
  # title = sprintf("Posterior Predictive Fits for County %s",i)
  title = sprintf("%s County",counties[i])
  
  post_pred_plot = preds %>% ggplot(aes(x=time,y=deaths)) + geom_point() +
    geom_line(aes(y=mean),color = "red") + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = `0.025quant`,ymax = `0.975quant`), fill = "orange",alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pplower,ymax = y_ppupper),fill = "skyblue",alpha = 0.3) + ggtitle(title) + theme_classic()
  print(post_pred_plot)
}

# kgr_model1_response_fit$fitted_values %>% group_by(county) %>% summarize(avg_width = mean(interval_width))
```

### Using K_time (AR6)

```{r}
kgr_model2_response_nb = function(dataset,link=1){
  
  ###Fit INLA model 
  kgr_formula2 = deaths ~ -1 + offset(log(Total_Pop)) + month + cum_monthly_total_pm25_2*Cluster + pm25 + total_precip + avg_air_temp + avg_dew_point + avg_wind_speed + f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))
  
  #Switch to f(id2,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = inv_covGP2_treatment) for true K_time
  
  model = inla(formula = kgr_formula2,family = "nbinomial",data = dataset, num.threads = 20,
                  control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE, config = TRUE,
                                         return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                  #control.family = list(variant = 1), E = Total_Pop,
                  control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                  control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                  control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = link))
  
  ###Extract relevant information and store in the list
  model_summary <- model$summary.fixed
  bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model)
  model_DIC <- model$dic$dic
  model_WAIC <- model$waic$waic
  preds_model <- model$summary.fitted.values
  preds_model <- cbind(dataset$NAME, dataset$time, dataset$ID, dataset$deaths, preds_model)
  colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")
  
  pp_ysamples = list()
  
  ###NBINOMIAL SAMPLING
  
  n_density = data.frame(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  sample_n = inla.rmarginal(1000,inla.smarginal(n_density))
  pp_nsamples = sample_n
  
  ###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
  for (i in 1:nrow(preds_model)){
    ###Sample 1000 mu for each row
    row = data.frame(model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])

    sample_mu = inla.rmarginal(1000,inla.smarginal(row))
    lower = quantile(sample_mu,0.025)
    mean = mean(sample_mu)
    upper = quantile(sample_mu,0.975)

    ###Sample from nbinom with these a and b
    sample_y = rnbinom(n = 1000, size = sample_n, mu = sample_mu)
    pp_ysamples[[i]] = sample_y
    
    ###Obtain 95% credible interval
    preds_model$y_ppmean[i] = mean(sample_y, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_pplower[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)
    preds_model$y_ppupper[i] = quantile(sample_y, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  preds_model$interval_width = preds_model$`0.975quant` - preds_model$`0.025quant` 
  # marginal_fvs <- model$marginals.fitted.values

  # #Exponentiating parameter to get better interpretation of estimates 
  # multeff <- exp(model$summary.fixed$mean)
  # names(multeff) <- model$names.fixed
  # 
  # #Plot of each parameters' posterior density 
  # mf <- melt(model$marginals.fixed)
  # cf <- spread(mf,Var2,value)
  # names(cf)[2] <- 'parameter'
  # param_plot = ggplot(cf,aes(x=x,y=y)) + geom_line()+facet_wrap(~ parameter, 
  #            scales="free") + geom_vline(xintercept=0) + ylab("density")
  
  #Plot of precision of random effect (main hyperparameter of interest)
  # sden <- data.frame(bri.hyper.sd(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]]))
  # hyperparam_plot = ggplot(sden,aes(x,y)) + geom_line() + ylab("density") + 
  #            xlab("linear predictor")
  
  #Store the results in the list
  kgr_model2_results = list(
    covmatrix = covGP2_response,
    prec = inv_covGP2_response,
    model_summary = model_summary,
    bri_hyperpar_summary = bri_hyperpar_summary,
    #exp_effects = multeff,
    #param_plot = param_plot,
    #hyperparam_plot = hyperparam_plot,
    model_DIC = model_DIC,
    model_WAIC = model_WAIC,
    fitted_values = preds_model,
    pp_ysamples = pp_ysamples,
    pp_nsamples = pp_nsamples
  )
  
  return(kgr_model2_results)
}

#Fit kgr_model2
kgr_model2_response_fit = kgr_model2_response_nb(dataset = inla_df_train)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model2_DIC = kgr_model2_response_fit$model_DIC
kgr_model2_WAIC = kgr_model2_response_fit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model2_response_fit$model_summary
kgr_model2_response_fit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model2_response_fit$exp_effects

#Show plots
kgr_model2_response_fit$param_plot
kgr_model2_response_fit$hyperparam_plot


for (i in 1:58){
  df = inla_df_train %>% filter(ID == i) %>% dplyr::select(deaths)
  preds = kgr_model2_response_fit$fitted_values %>% filter(ID == i)
  df = cbind(df,preds)
  
  # title = sprintf("Posterior Predictive Fits for County %s",i)
  title = sprintf("%s County",counties[i])
  
  post_pred_plot = preds %>% ggplot(aes(x=time,y=deaths)) + geom_point() +
    geom_line(aes(y=mean),color = "red") + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = `0.025quant`,ymax = `0.975quant`), fill = "orange",alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pplower,ymax = y_ppupper),fill = "skyblue",alpha = 0.3) + ggtitle(title) + theme_classic()
  print(post_pred_plot)
}

# kgr_model2_response_fit$fitted_values %>% group_by(county) %>% summarize(avg_width = mean(interval_width))
```

# Evaluate out of sample performance

## Attach identical dataset except with response omitted - estimate model then make predictions for 2019

Alternative out of sample dataset: 

```{r}
inla_df2 = inla_df

out_idx = which(inla_df2$time >= 36 & inla_df2$time <= 48)
out_idx = c(out_idx,which(inla_df2$time >= 60))

#Switch to specific time period
inla_df2$deaths[out_idx] = NA
```

## KGR models

### Using K_cov

```{r,cache=TRUE}
#Fit kgr_model1
kgr_model1_response_outfit = kgr_model1_response_nb(dataset = inla_df2)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model1_DIC = kgr_model1_response_outfit$model_DIC
kgr_model1_WAIC = kgr_model1_response_outfit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model1_response_outfit$model_summary
kgr_model1_response_outfit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model1_response_outfit$exp_effects

#Show plots
kgr_model1_response_outfit$param_plot
kgr_model1_response_outfit$hyperparam_plot

plot_counties = c('Fresno','Modoc','Humboldt','San Diego','Alameda')

plot_list = list()
tracker=1

for (i in plot_counties){
  df = inla_df %>% filter(NAME == i) %>% dplyr::select(deaths)
  preds = kgr_model1_response_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county == i) 
  preds$deaths = df$deaths
  
  # title = sprintf("Posterior Predictive Fits for County %s",i)
  title = sprintf("%s County - SDI Group %d",i,tracker)
  
  post_pred_plot = preds %>% ggplot(aes(x = time, y = deaths)) + 
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = `0.025quant`, ymax = `0.975quant`), fill = "#BDBDBD", alpha = 0.9) + 
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pplower, ymax = y_ppupper), fill = "#E0E0E0", alpha = 0.5) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line(aes(y = mean), color = "#2166AC") + 
    
    # Shaded regions for out-of-sample predictions
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = 36, xmax = 48, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf), fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.002) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = 60, xmax = max(time), ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf), fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.002) +
  
    # Vertical lines for reference
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(36,48), linetype = "dashed", color = "#636363", linewidth = 1.5) + 
    geom_vline(xintercept = 60, linetype = "dashed", color = "#636363", linewidth = 1.5) + 
    theme_classic() + 
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 72, by = 12),labels = 2014:2019) + 
    labs(x = "Year", y = "Mortality",title = title) + 
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12))
  
  print(post_pred_plot)
  
  plot_list[[tracker]] = post_pred_plot
  tracker=tracker+1
}

combined = wrap_plots(plotlist = plot_list, nrow = 2)

# Save one combined figure
# ggsave(
#   filename = paste0("outcome_preds_combined_plot.png"),
#   plot = combined,
#   path = "/home/jovyan/work/important-plots",
#   width = 15,
#   height = 10,
#   dpi = 600
# )

# kgr_model1_response_outfit$fitted_values %>% group_by(county) %>% summarize(avg_width = mean(interval_width))
```

## Attach identical dataset except with treatment omitted - estimate model then make predictions for 2019

```{r}
inla_df3 = inla_df

#Switch to specific time period

```

## KGR models

### Using K_cov

```{r,cache=TRUE}
#Fit kgr_model1
kgr_model1_treatment_outfit = kgr_model1_treatment(dataset = inla_df3)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model1_DIC = kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$model_DIC
kgr_model1_WAIC = kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates

kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$exp_effects

#Show plots
kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$param_plot
kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$hyperparam_plot

exposure_label = expression(paste("2 month accumulation of smoke PM2.5 ("*mu*"g/m"^3*")"))

for (i in plot_counties){
  df = inla_df %>% filter(NAME == i) %>% dplyr::select(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)
  preds = kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county == i) 
  preds$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 = df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2
  
  # title = sprintf("Posterior Predictive Fits for County %s",i)
  title = sprintf("%s County",counties[i])
  
  post_pred_plot = preds %>% ggplot(aes(x = time, y = cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)) + 
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = `0.025quant`, ymax = `0.975quant`), fill = "#BDBDBD", alpha = 0.9) + 
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pplower, ymax = y_ppupper), fill = "#E0E0E0", alpha = 0.5) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line(aes(y = mean), color = "#2166AC") + 
    
    # Shaded regions for out-of-sample predictions
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = 36, xmax = 48, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf), fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.002) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = 60, xmax = max(time), ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf), fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.002) +
  
    # Vertical lines for reference
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(36,48), linetype = "dashed", color = "#636363", linewidth = 1.5) + 
    geom_vline(xintercept = 60, linetype = "dashed", color = "#636363", linewidth = 1.5) + 
    theme_classic() + 
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 72, by = 12),labels = 2014:2019) + 
    labs(x = "Year", y = exposure_label,title = title) + 
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12))
  
  print(post_pred_plot)
}
# kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$fitted_values %>% group_by(county) %>% summarize(avg_width = mean(interval_width))
```

```{r,cache=TRUE}
plot_list = list()
tracker=1

for (i in plot_counties){
  df = inla_df %>% filter(NAME == i) %>% dplyr::select(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)
  preds = kgr_model2_treatment_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county == i) 
  preds$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 = df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2
  
  # title = sprintf("Posterior Predictive Fits for County %s",i)
  title = sprintf("%s County - SDI Group %d",i,tracker)
  
  post_pred_plot = preds %>% ggplot(aes(x = time, y = cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)) + 
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = `0.025quant`, ymax = `0.975quant`), fill = "#BDBDBD", alpha = 0.9) + 
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pplower, ymax = y_ppupper), fill = "#E0E0E0", alpha = 0.5) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line(aes(y = mean), color = "#2166AC") + 
    
    # Shaded regions for out-of-sample predictions
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = 36, xmax = 48, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf), fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.002) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = 60, xmax = max(time), ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf), fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.002) +
  
    # Vertical lines for reference
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(36,48), linetype = "dashed", color = "#636363", linewidth = 1.5) + 
    geom_vline(xintercept = 60, linetype = "dashed", color = "#636363", linewidth = 1.5) + 
    theme_classic() + 
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 72, by = 12),labels = 2014:2019) + 
    coord_cartesian(ylim=c(0,5000)) +
    labs(x = "Year", y = exposure_label,title = title) + 
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12))
  
  print(post_pred_plot)
  
  plot_list[[tracker]] = post_pred_plot
  tracker=tracker+1
}
```

```{r,cache=TRUE}
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = 60, xmax = max(time), ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf), fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.002) +
```

## Calculate out of sample forecast accuracy for treatment and outcome

### Outcome

```{r}
actual_deaths = inla_df$deaths[out_idx]

kgr1_deaths_preds = kgr_model1_response_outfit$fitted_values$mean[out_idx]
kgr2_deaths_preds = kgr_model2_response_outfit$fitted_values$mean[out_idx]

test_rmse1 = sqrt(mean((actual_deaths - kgr1_deaths_preds)^2))
test_rmse2 = sqrt(mean((actual_deaths - kgr2_deaths_preds)^2))

test_rmse1
test_rmse2
```

### Treatment

```{r}
actual_exposure = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[out_idx]

kgr1_exposure_preds = kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$fitted_values$zhat[out_idx]
kgr2_exposure_preds = kgr_model2_treatment_outfit$fitted_values$zhat[out_idx]
gamma_kgr2_exposure_preds = gamma_treatment_outfit$fitted_values$mean[out_idx]

test_rmse3 = sqrt(mean((actual_exposure - kgr1_exposure_preds)^2))
test_rmse4 = sqrt(mean((actual_exposure - kgr2_exposure_preds)^2))
test_rmse5 = sqrt(mean((actual_exposure - gamma_kgr2_exposure_preds)^2))

test_rmse3
test_rmse4
test_rmse5
```

## Calculate credible interval band width for treatment and outcome

### Outcome

```{r}
mean(kgr_model1_response_outfit$fitted_values$interval_width[out_idx])
mean(kgr_model2_response_outfit$fitted_values$interval_width[out_idx])
```

### Treatment

```{r}
mean(kgr_model1_treatment_outfit$fitted_values$interval_width[out_idx])
mean(kgr_model2_treatment_outfit$fitted_values$interval_width[out_idx])
mean(gamma_treatment_outfit$fitted_values$interval_width[out_idx])
```

## Calculate coverage tables for treatment and outcome

### Treatment

In and out of sample:

```{r}
### County level
coverage_county = rep(0,58)

for (i in 1:58){
  county_idx = which(inla_df$NAME == CA_counties[i])
  true_treatment = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[county_idx]
  treatment_preds = kgr_model2_treatment_outfit$fitted_values[county_idx,] #change model if necessary
  
  lci = treatment_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
  lci = lci$y_pplower - 0.0001
  
  uci = treatment_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
  uci = uci$y_ppupper - 0.0001
  
  captured = (true_treatment >= lci & true_treatment <= uci)
  coverage_county[i] = mean(captured)
}

#Overall 
coverage_overall = 0
true_treatment = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2

all_lci = kgr_model2_treatment_outfit$fitted_values %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
all_lci = all_lci$y_pplower - 0.0001
  
all_uci = kgr_model2_treatment_outfit$fitted_values %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
all_uci = all_uci$y_ppupper - 0.0001

captured = (true_treatment >= all_lci & true_treatment <= all_uci)
coverage_overall = mean(captured)


#Show results
coverage_county = cbind(CA_counties,coverage_county)
coverage_county

coverage_overall
```

Just out of sample:

```{r}
true_vals = inla_df[out_idx,]
inla_fits = kgr_model2_treatment_outfit$fitted_values[out_idx,] #change model if necessary

### County level
coverage_county2 = rep(0,58)

for (i in 1:58){
  county_idx = which(true_vals$NAME == CA_counties[i])
  true_treatment = true_vals$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[county_idx]
  treatment_preds = inla_fits[county_idx,] 
  
  lci = treatment_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
  lci = lci$y_pplower - 0.0001
  
  uci = treatment_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
  uci = uci$y_ppupper - 0.0001
  
  captured = (true_treatment >= lci & true_treatment <= uci)
  coverage_county2[i] = mean(captured)
}

#Overall 
coverage_overall2 = 0
true_treatment = true_vals$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2

all_lci = inla_fits %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
all_lci = all_lci$y_pplower - 0.0001
  
all_uci = inla_fits %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
all_uci = all_uci$y_ppupper - 0.0001

captured = (true_treatment >= all_lci & true_treatment <= all_uci)
coverage_overall2 = mean(captured)


#Show results
coverage_county2 = cbind(CA_counties,coverage_county2)
coverage_county2

coverage_overall2
```

Gamma results:

In and out of sample:

```{r}
### County level
coverage_county = rep(0,58)

for (i in 1:58){
  county_idx = which(inla_df$NAME == CA_counties[i])
  true_treatment = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[county_idx]
  treatment_preds = gamma_treatment_outfit$fitted_values[county_idx,] #change model if necessary
  
  lci = treatment_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
  lci = lci$y_pplower - 0.0001
  
  uci = treatment_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
  uci = uci$y_ppupper - 0.0001
  
  captured = (true_treatment >= lci & true_treatment <= uci)
  coverage_county[i] = mean(captured)
}

#Overall

true_treatment = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2
```
