Just out of sample:

```{r}
true_vals = inla_df[out_idx,]
inla_fits = gamma_treatment_outfit$fitted_values[out_idx,] #change model if necessary

### County level
coverage_county2 = rep(0,58)

for (i in 1:58){
  county_idx = which(true_vals$NAME == CA_counties[i])
  true_treatment = true_vals$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[county_idx]
  treatment_preds = inla_fits[county_idx,] 
  
  lci = treatment_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
  lci = lci$y_pplower - 0.0001
  
  uci = treatment_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
  uci = uci$y_ppupper - 0.0001
  
  captured = (true_treatment >= lci & true_treatment <= uci)
  coverage_county2[i] = mean(captured)
}

#Overall 
coverage_overall2 = 0
true_treatment = true_vals$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2

all_lci = inla_fits %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
all_lci = all_lci$y_pplower - 0.0001
  
all_uci = inla_fits %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
all_uci = all_uci$y_ppupper - 0.0001

captured = (true_treatment >= all_lci & true_treatment <= all_uci)
coverage_overall2 = mean(captured)


#Show results
coverage_county2 = cbind(CA_counties,coverage_county2)
coverage_county2

coverage_overall2
```

### Outcome

In and out of sample:

```{r}
### County level
coverage_county3 = rep(0,58)

for (i in 1:58){
  county_idx = which(inla_df$NAME == CA_counties[i])
  true_outcome = inla_df$deaths[county_idx]
  outcome_preds = kgr_model1_response_outfit$fitted_values[county_idx,] #change model if necessary
  
  lci = outcome_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
  uci = outcome_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
  
  captured = (true_outcome >= lci$y_pplower & true_outcome <= uci$y_ppupper)
  coverage_county3[i] = mean(captured)
}

#Overall 
coverage_overall3 = 0
true_outcome = inla_df$deaths

all_lci = kgr_model1_response_outfit$fitted_values %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
all_uci = kgr_model1_response_outfit$fitted_values %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)

captured = (true_outcome >= all_lci$y_pplower & true_outcome <= all_uci$y_ppupper)
coverage_overall3 = mean(captured)


#Show results
coverage_county3 = cbind(CA_counties,coverage_county3)
coverage_county3

coverage_overall3
```

Just out of sample:

```{r}
true_vals = inla_df[out_idx,]
inla_fits = kgr_model1_response_outfit$fitted_values[out_idx,]

### County level
coverage_county4 = rep(0,58)

for (i in 1:58){
  county_idx = which(true_vals$NAME == CA_counties[i])
  true_outcome = true_vals$deaths[county_idx]
  outcome_preds = inla_fits[county_idx,] #change model if necessary
  
  lci = outcome_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
  uci = outcome_preds %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)
  
  captured = (true_outcome >= lci$y_pplower & true_outcome <= uci$y_ppupper)
  coverage_county4[i] = sum(captured)/length(captured)
}

#Overall 
coverage_overall4 = 0
true_outcome = true_vals$deaths

all_lci = inla_fits %>% dplyr::select(y_pplower)
all_uci = inla_fits %>% dplyr::select(y_ppupper)

captured = (true_outcome >= all_lci$y_pplower & true_outcome <= all_uci$y_ppupper)
coverage_overall4 = mean(captured)


#Show results
coverage_county4 = cbind(CA_counties,coverage_county4)
coverage_county4

coverage_overall4
```

# Causal inference with MSMs

Note: we use covariate history instead of instantaneous values in case the confounders dominate the signal

## GLM outcome model

```{r}
library(MASS) 

#Create scaled version of exposure
inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled = (inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 - mean(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)) / sd(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)

#Weighted NB regression
# causal_glm1 = glm.nb(deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop)) + factor(month) + lead + co + so2 + no2 + o3 + pm10 + nonsmoke_pm25 + cum_total_precip_2 + avg_avg_air_temp_2 + avg_avg_dew_point_2 + max_avg_wind_speed_2 +  cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled + I(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled^2) + I(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled^3), weights = sw_prod2, data = inla_df)

causal_glm1 = glm.nb(deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop)) + factor(month) + ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled,df=3), 
                     weights = sw_prod, data = inla_df)

summary(causal_glm1)

# NB regression with SWs included as covariate
# causal_glm2 = glm.nb(deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop)) + factor(month) + lead + co + so2 + no2 + o3 + pm10 + nonsmoke_pm25 + cum_total_precip_2 + avg_avg_air_temp_2 + avg_avg_dew_point_2 + max_avg_wind_speed_2 +  cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled + I(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled^2) + I(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled^3) + sw_prod2, data = inla_df)

causal_glm2 = glm.nb(deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop)) + factor(month) + ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled,df=3) + 
                       sw_prod2, data = inla_df)

summary(causal_glm2)

detach("package:MASS", unload = TRUE) 
```

Interpretation for GLM 1:

cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled coefficients are not statistically significant

Interpretation for GLM 2:

cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled coefficient = -0.0328244 -\> exp(-0.0328244) = 0.9677085

cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled\^2 coefficient = 0.0060388 -\> exp(0.0060388) = 1.006057

-\>

Weakly significant relationship (negative and nonlinear)

```{r}
# coefficients from your fitted model
b1 <- -0.0328244
b2 <-  0.0060388

# mean and sd used in scaling
pm_mean <- 62.60041
pm_sd   <- 177.3759

# grid on ORIGINAL PM scale
pm_grid <- 0:500

# scaled values
Z  <- (pm_grid - pm_mean) / pm_sd
Z0 <- 0   # reference = mean exposure

# rate ratio vs mean exposure
RR <- exp(b1 * (Z - Z0) + b2 * (Z^2 - Z0^2))

df_curve <- data.frame(
  cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 = pm_grid,
  RR_vs_mean = RR
)

p = ggplot(df_curve,
       aes(x = cum_monthly_total_pm25_2, y = RR_vs_mean)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(
    x = "Cumulative monthly smoke PM2.5",
    y = "Rate ratio (vs mean exposure)",
    title = "Implied quadratic exposure–response from reference GLM"
  ) +
  theme_classic() 

# ggsave(p, path = "~/work/important-plots",filename = "msm-drf.png",dpi = 600)
```

## Basic KGR outcome model

```{r,cache=TRUE}
###Fit INLA model 
# kgr_formula2 = deaths ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + factor(month) + aqi_resids + cum_total_precip_6 + avg_avg_air_temp_6 + avg_avg_dew_point_6 + max_avg_wind_speed_6 +  cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled + I(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled^2) + sw3 + f(id2,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = inv_covGP2)

kgr_formula2 = deaths ~ -1 + offset(log(Total_Pop)) + factor(month) + lead + co + so2 + no2 + o3 + pm10 + nonsmoke_pm25 + cum_total_precip_2 + avg_avg_air_temp_2 + avg_avg_dew_point_2 + max_avg_wind_speed_2 +  Group*cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled + sw_prod2 + f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))

model = inla(formula = kgr_formula2,family = "nbinomial",data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,
                                       return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                #control.family = list(variant = 1), E = Total_Pop,
                control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

###Extract relevant information and store in the list
model$summary.fixed

# model_summary <- model$summary.fixed
# bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model)
# model_DIC <- model$dic$dic
# model_WAIC <- model$waic$waic
# preds_model <- model$summary.fitted.values
# preds_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$deaths, preds_model)
# colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")
```

Interpretation:

cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_scaled coefficient is not significant: -3.035114e-02 -\> exp(-3.035114e-02) = 0.9701

## Plotting monthly effects

```{r}
m_beta = model$summary.fixed[1:12,1]
month = c("January","February","March","April","May","June","July",
          "August","September","October","November","December")
monthly_fe = data.frame(month = factor(month, levels = month.name),m_beta = m_beta, value = exp(m_beta))


ggplot(monthly_fe, aes(x = month, y = value)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Monthly Fixed Effects", x = "Month", y = "Value") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

# Modeling a nonlinear function between treatment and outcome (DRF)

Setup:

```{r}
inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_binned = inla.group(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,n=10,method = "cut")

inla_df$Cluster = as.numeric(inla_df$Cluster)
inla_df$Group = as.numeric(inla_df$Group)

inla_df$Group2 = relevel(factor(inla_df$Group), "4") #set baseline to 4? 

# inla_df$pm25_dev = inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_binned
inla_df$pm25_dev = inla.group(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,n=10,method = "cut")

inla_df$sw_binned = inla.group(inla_df$sw_prod,n=20,method = "quantile")


prior_fixed = list(mean = list(default = 0), 
                   prec = list(default = 0.001,`Group21:1` = 10,
                               `Group22:1` = 10,`Group23:1` = 10,
                               `Group25:1` = 10,`Group21:2` = 10,
                               `Group22:2` = 10,`Group23:2` = 10,
                               `Group25:2` = 10,`Group21:3` = 10,
                               `Group22:3` = 10,`Group23:3` = 10,
                               `Group25:3` = 10))

z_knots = c(1,50)
# z_knots = c(1,quantile(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,c(0.7,0.9)))
# z_knots = c(1,25,50,150)
```

```{r,cache=TRUE}
kgr_formula_hierarchical = deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop/100000)) +
        f(NAME,model = "iid",hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "loggamma", param = c(10, 5)))) +
        factor(month) + 
        sw_prod + I(sw_prod^2) + I(sw_prod^3) + 
        # Spline for treatment * SDI group 
        Group2*ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_binned,df = 3) +
        f(ID,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar",order = 6))

kgr_formula_hierarchical2 <- deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop/100000)) +
  factor(month) + 
  f(time,model = "iid",hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec",param = c(0.5,0.01))),constr=TRUE) + 
  sw_prod2 + I(sw_prod2^2) + I(sw_prod2^3) +
  f(NAME, model = "iid",constr=TRUE) + 
  ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,df=3) + 
  f(pm25_dev, model = "rw2", replicate = Group, scale.model = TRUE, constr=TRUE,
    hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(0.05, 0.5)))) +
  f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), 
    group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))

tic()
model3 = inla(formula = kgr_formula_hierarchical2,family = "nbinomial",data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,
                                       return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                #control.fixed = prior_fixed,
                control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))
toc()

model_summary <- model3$summary.fixed
bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model3)
model_DIC <- model3$dic$dic
model_WAIC <- model3$waic$waic
preds_model <- model3$summary.fitted.values
preds_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$deaths, preds_model)
colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

final_model_results = list(
  model_summary = model_summary,
  bri_hyperpar_summary = bri_hyperpar_summary,
  model_DIC = model_DIC,
  model_WAIC = model_WAIC,
  fitted_values = preds_model
)


model3$summary.fixed
model3$summary.hyperpar
model3$summary.random$pm25_dev
```

### Visualize spline curve

```{r}
# Extract Z
Z <- inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2

# Rebuild spline basis (same as model)
B <- ns(Z,knots = z_knots)

# Get fixed effects
fixef <- model3$summary.fixed

# Find spline rows
fixef_spline <- fixef[16:18, ]

beta_hat <- fixef_spline$mean

# Estimated spline contribution
spline_effect <- as.vector(B %*% beta_hat)

plot_df <- data.frame(
  Z = Z,
  deaths = inla_df$deaths,
  spline = spline_effect
)

plot_df <- plot_df %>% arrange(Z)

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Z)) +
  geom_line(
    aes(y = spline),
    color = "blue",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Cumulative PM2.5",
    y = "Log Relative Risk",
    title = "Spline Effect of Z (cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)"
  ) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,200)) + 
  theme_bw()
```

### Posterior predictive check for zero inflation/overdispersion

```{r,cache=TRUE}
zip_formula = deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop/100000)) +
  factor(month) + 
  f(time,model = "iid",hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec",param = c(0.5,0.01))),constr=TRUE) + 
  sw_prod2 + I(sw_prod2^2) + I(sw_prod2^3) +
  f(NAME, model = "iid",constr=TRUE) + 
  ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,df=3) + 
  f(pm25_dev, model = "rw2", replicate = Group, scale.model = TRUE, constr=TRUE,
    hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(0.05, 0.5)))) +
  f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), 
    group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))

###Try zeroinflatedpoisson0, zeroinflatedpoisson1, zeroinflatednbinomial0, zeroinflatednbinomial1
zip_model = inla(formula = zip_formula,family = "zeroinflatedpoisson0",data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                #control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

preds_zip_model <- zip_model$summary.fitted.values
preds_zip_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$deaths, preds_zip_model)
colnames(preds_zip_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")
```

```{r}
kgr_formula_hierarchical2 <- deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop/100000)) +
  factor(month) + 
  f(time,model = "iid",hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec",param = c(0.5,0.01))),constr=TRUE) + 
  sw_prod2 + I(sw_prod2^2) + I(sw_prod2^3) +
  f(NAME, model = "iid",constr=TRUE) + 
  ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,df=3) + 
  f(pm25_dev, model = "rw2", replicate = Group, scale.model = TRUE, constr=TRUE,
    hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(0.05, 0.5)))) +
  f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), 
    group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))

pois_model = inla(formula = kgr_formula_hierarchical2,family = "poisson",data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,cpo = TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                #control.fixed = prior_fixed,
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))
```

```{r}
###Posterior predictive sampling (ZIP)
p_density = data.frame(zip_model$marginals.hyperpar$`zero-probability parameter for zero-inflated poisson_0`)
sample_p = inla.rmarginal(100000,inla.smarginal(p_density)) 
pp_psamples = sample_p  

pp_zip_samples = list()


###RENDER ERROR HERE???
###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
for (i in 1:nrow(preds_zip_model)){ 
  ###Sample 1000 mu for each row
  row = data.frame(zip_model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])

  sample_lambda = inla.rmarginal(100000,inla.smarginal(row))
  lower = quantile(sample_lambda,0.025)
  mean = mean(sample_lambda)
  upper = quantile(sample_lambda,0.975)

  sample_y = ifelse(rbinom(100000, size = 1, prob = sample_p) > 0, 0, rpois(100000, lambda = sample_lambda))
  pp_zip_samples[[i]] = sample_y
}

###Poisson
preds_pois_model <- pois_model$summary.fitted.values
preds_pois_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$deaths, preds_pois_model)
colnames(preds_pois_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

###Posterior predictive sampling (Poisson)
pp_pois_samples = list()

###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
for (i in 1:nrow(preds_pois_model)){
    ###Sample 1000 mu for each row
    row = data.frame(pois_model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])
  
    sample_lambda = inla.rmarginal(100000,inla.smarginal(row))
    lower = quantile(sample_lambda,0.025)
    mean = mean(sample_lambda)
    upper = quantile(sample_lambda,0.975)
  
    sample_y = rpois(n=100000,lambda = sample_lambda)
    pp_pois_samples[[i]] = sample_y
}


###Posterior predictive sampling (NB)
pp_nb_samples = list()

n_density = data.frame(model3$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
sample_n = inla.rmarginal(1000,inla.smarginal(n_density))
pp_nsamples = sample_n  

###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
for (i in 1:nrow(preds_model)){
  ###Sample 1000 mu for each row
  row = data.frame(model3$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])

  sample_mu = inla.rmarginal(1000,inla.smarginal(row))
  lower = quantile(sample_mu,0.025)
  mean = mean(sample_mu)
  upper = quantile(sample_mu,0.975)

  ###Sample from nbinom with these a and b
  sample_y = rnbinom(n = 1000, size = sample_n, mu = sample_mu)
  pp_nb_samples[[i]] = sample_y
}
```

```{r}
###PPC
true_prop0 = length(which(inla_df$deaths == 0)) / nrow(inla_df)
true_mv = mean(inla_df$deaths) / var(inla_df$deaths)

###ZIP
pp_zip_sample_df = do.call(rbind,pp_zip_samples)

zip_prop0 = c()
zip_mv = c()

for(i in 1:1000){
  deaths = pp_zip_sample_df[,i]
  zip_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  zip_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths)
}


###POISSON
pp_pois_sample_df = do.call(rbind,pp_pois_samples)

pois_prop0 = c()
pois_mv = c()

for(i in 1:1000){
  deaths = pp_pois_sample_df[,i]
  pois_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  pois_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths)
}


###NB
pp_nb_sample_df = do.call(rbind,pp_nb_samples)

nb_prop0 = c()
nb_mv = c()

for(i in 1:1000){
  deaths = pp_nb_sample_df[,i]
  nb_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  nb_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths)
}

# hist(zip_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "ZIP Prop 0")
# abline(v = true_prop0)
# 
# hist(pois_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "Poisson Prop 0")
# abline(v = true_prop0)
# 
# 
# hist(zip_mv,xlim = c(0.006,0.008),main = "ZIP Mean/Var")
# abline(v = true_mv)
# 
# hist(pois_mv,xlim = c(0.006,0.008),main = "Poisson Mean/Var")
# abline(v = true_mv)

df_zip_prop0  = data.frame(value = zip_prop0)
df_pois_prop0 = data.frame(value = pois_prop0)
df_nb_prop0 = data.frame(value = nb_prop0)

df_zip_mv  = data.frame(value = zip_mv)
df_pois_mv = data.frame(value = pois_mv)
df_nb_mv = data.frame(value = nb_mv)

#Proportion of 0 plots
p_zip_prop0 <- ggplot(df_zip_prop0, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, boundary = 0, closed = "left") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = true_prop0, linewidth = 0.9) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.1, 0.3)) +
  labs(
    title = "ZIP: Proportion of Zeros",
    x = "Prop(Deaths = 0)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_pois_prop0 <- ggplot(df_pois_prop0, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, boundary = 0, closed = "left") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = true_prop0, linewidth = 0.9) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.1, 0.3)) +
  labs(
    title = "Poisson: Proportion of Zeros",
    x = "Prop(Deaths = 0)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_nb_prop0 <- ggplot(df_nb_prop0, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, boundary = 0, closed = "left") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = true_prop0, linewidth = 0.9) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.1, 0.3)) +
  labs(
    title = "Negative Binomial: Proportion of Zeros",
    x = "Prop(Deaths = 0)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


#Mean/Variance plots
p_zip_mv <- ggplot(df_zip_mv, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, boundary = 0, closed = "left") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = true_mv, linewidth = 0.9) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.006, 0.008)) +
  labs(
    title = "ZIP: Mean / Variance",
    x = "Mean(Deaths) / Var(Deaths)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_pois_mv <- ggplot(df_pois_mv, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, boundary = 0, closed = "left") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = true_mv, linewidth = 0.9) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.006, 0.008)) +
  labs(
    title = "Poisson: Mean / Variance",
    x = "Mean(Deaths) / Var(Deaths)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_nb_mv <- ggplot(df_nb_mv, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, boundary = 0, closed = "left") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = true_mv, linewidth = 0.9) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.006, 0.008)) +
  labs(
    title = "Negative Binomial: Mean / Variance",
    x = "Mean(Deaths) / Var(Deaths)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#Combine panels
prop0_panel <- p_zip_prop0 + p_pois_prop0
mv_panel <- p_pois_mv + p_nb_mv

#Save figures
# ggsave(
#   filename = "~/work/important-plots/ppc_prop0.png",
#   prop0_panel,
#   width = 12,
#   height = 4,
#   dpi = 600
# )

# ggsave(
#   filename = "~/work/important-plots/ppc_meanvar.png",
#   mv_panel,
#   width = 12,
#   height = 4,
#   dpi = 600
# )

prop0_panel
mv_panel
```

```{r}
###Split into groups to parse signal
small_counties = na.omit(counties[which(CApops$POPESTIMATE2019 <= 100000)])
small_idx = which(inla_df$NAME %in% small_counties)
large_idx = which(!(inla_df$NAME %in% small_counties))

inla_df_small = inla_df[small_idx,]
inla_df_large = inla_df[large_idx,]

###Small
small_prop0 = length(which(inla_df_small$deaths == 0)) / nrow(inla_df_small)
small_mv = mean(inla_df_small$deaths) / var(inla_df_small$deaths)

zip_small_prop0 = c()
zip_small_mv = c()

for(i in 1:100000){
  deaths = pp_zip_sample_df[small_idx,i]
  zip_small_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  zip_small_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths) 
}


pois_small_prop0 = c()
pois_small_mv = c()

for(i in 1:100000){
  deaths = pp_pois_sample_df[small_idx,i]
  pois_small_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  pois_small_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths) 
}

hist(zip_small_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "ZIP Prop 0 (Small Counties)")
abline(v = small_prop0)

hist(pois_small_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "Poisson Prop 0 (Small Counties)")
abline(v = small_prop0)


hist(zip_small_mv,xlim = c(0.004,0.006),main = "ZIP Mean/Var (Small Counties)")
abline(v = small_mv)

hist(pois_small_mv,xlim = c(0.004,0.006),main = "Poisson Mean/Var (Small Counties)")
abline(v = small_mv)

###Large
large_prop0 = length(which(inla_df_large$deaths == 0)) / nrow(inla_df_large)
large_mv = mean(inla_df_large$deaths) / var(inla_df_large$deaths)

zip_large_prop0 = c()
zip_large_mv = c()

for(i in 1:100000){
  deaths = pp_zip_sample_df[large_idx,i]
  zip_large_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  zip_large_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths) 
}


pois_large_prop0 = c()
pois_large_mv = c()

for(i in 1:100000){
  deaths = pp_pois_sample_df[large_idx,i]
  pois_large_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  pois_large_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths) 
}

hist(zip_large_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "ZIP Prop 0 (Large Counties)")
abline(v = large_prop0)

hist(pois_large_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "Poisson Prop 0 (Large Counties)")
abline(v = large_prop0)


hist(zip_large_mv,xlim = c(0.02,0.025),main = "ZIP Mean/Var (Large Counties)")
abline(v = large_mv)

hist(pois_large_mv,xlim = c(0.02,0.025),main = "Poisson Mean/Var (Large Counties)")
abline(v = large_mv)
```

### Negative Binomial PPC

#### CPO

$CPO_i = p(y_i \mid \bf{y}_{-i}) \approx \frac{1}{E_{\theta \mid \bf{y}}[1/p(y_i / \theta)]}$

```{r}
#CPO
final_outcome_cpo_vals = model3$cpo$cpo
mean(final_outcome_cpo_vals)

sum(final_outcome_cpo_vals > 0)  
sum(model3$cpo$failure > 0)
bad_obs <- which(final_outcome_cpo_vals < 0.01)
length(bad_obs)/length(final_outcome_cpo_vals)

# plot(final_outcome_cpo_vals, ylab="CPO", xlab="Observation index")
# abline(h=0.01, col="red", lty=2)

outcome_ppc_df = inla_df %>% select(ID,NAME,date,time,month,deaths,cum_monthly_total_pm25_2) %>% cbind(final_outcome_cpo_vals)

spatiotemporal_cpo2 = outcome_ppc_df %>% group_by(NAME,month) %>% summarize(bad_preds = length(which(final_outcome_cpo_vals <= 0.01)))

unique(spatiotemporal_cpo2$NAME[which(spatiotemporal_cpo2$bad_preds > 4)])

for (i in 1:58){
title = sprintf("CPO values for %s County",counties[i])

post_pred_plot = outcome_ppc_df %>% filter(ID == i) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=time,y=final_outcome_cpo_vals)) + geom_point() + 
  geom_line(aes(y=0.01),color = "red") + ggtitle(title) + 
  theme_classic()

print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

CPO values are better here - less outliers. Outliers tend to happen in first months of the year (makes sense if wildfire causes deaths a couple months later). Only Los Angeles has consistent outliers (for Jan-Apr).

```{r}
#CPO analysis by wildfire szn
wf_cpo_vals = final_outcome_cpo_vals[wildfire_idx]
nonwf_cpo_vals = final_outcome_cpo_vals[!wildfire_idx]

nonwf_cpo_outliers <- which(nonwf_cpo_vals < 0.01)
length(nonwf_cpo_outliers) / length(nonwf_cpo_vals)

wf_cpo_outliers <- which(wf_cpo_vals < 0.01)
length(wf_cpo_outliers) / length(wf_cpo_vals)
```

#### PIT

$PIT_i = P(Y_i^{rep} \leq y_i \mid \bf{y}_{-i}) = \int_{-\infty}^{y_i} p(y \mid \bf{y}_i) dy$

```{r}
#PIT
final_outcome_pit_vals = model3$cpo$pit

hist(final_outcome_pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line


df_pit <- data.frame(pit = final_outcome_pit_vals)

nb_pit_plot <- ggplot(df_pit, aes(x = pit)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    bins = 50,
    boundary = 0,
    closed = "left"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Negative Binomial PIT Histogram",
    x = "PIT",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

nb_pit_plot


ks.test(final_outcome_pit_vals, "punif")  # Kolmogorov-Smirnov test

outcome_ppc_df = cbind(outcome_ppc_df,final_outcome_pit_vals)

spatiotemporal_pit2 = outcome_ppc_df %>% group_by(NAME) %>% summarize(bad_dist = length(which(final_outcome_pit_vals > 0.5)))

# spatiotemporal_pit2 = outcome_ppc_df %>% group_by(NAME,month) %>% summarize(bad_dist = length(which(final_outcome_pit_vals > 0.5)))

counties[which((spatiotemporal_pit2$bad_dist/72) > 0.95)] #counties with systematic underprediction (entire dist has PIT values > 0.5)

for (i in 1:58){
title = sprintf("PIT values for %s County",counties[i])

post_pred_plot = outcome_ppc_df %>% filter(ID == i) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=time,y=final_outcome_pit_vals)) + geom_point() + 
  geom_line(aes(y=1),color = "red") + ggtitle(title) + 
  theme_classic()

print(post_pred_plot)
}
```

```{r}
#PIT analysis by wildfire szn
wf_pit_vals = final_outcome_pit_vals[wildfire_idx]
nonwf_pit_vals = final_outcome_pit_vals[!wildfire_idx]

hist(wf_pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line

hist(nonwf_pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line
```

#### RMSE + MAR

```{r}
wildfire_months = c(1,2,7:12)
wildfire_idx = inla_df$month %in% wildfire_months
```

```{r,cache=TRUE}
n_samples = 100

#RMSE PPC
yobs = inla_df$deaths
resids = yobs - model3$summary.fitted.values$mean

RMSE = sqrt(mean(resids^2))
MAR = max(abs(resids))
MAD = median(abs(resids))

obs_county_metrics = matrix(NA, nrow = 58, ncol = 3)
colnames(obs_county_metrics) = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")

for (k in 1:58){
  start = (k-1)*72 + 1
  end = k*72
  county_resids = resids[start:end]

  obs_county_metrics[k,1] = sqrt(mean(county_resids^2))
  obs_county_metrics[k,2] = max(abs(county_resids))
  obs_county_metrics[k,3] = median(abs(county_resids))
}

obs_wildfire_szn_metrics = matrix(NA, nrow = 2, ncol = 3)
colnames(obs_wildfire_szn_metrics) = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")

wildfire_szn_resids = resids[wildfire_idx]
nonwildfire_szn_resids = resids[!wildfire_idx]

obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[1,1] = sqrt(mean(wildfire_szn_resids^2))
obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[1,2] = max(abs(wildfire_szn_resids))
obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[1,3] = median(abs(wildfire_szn_resids))

obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[2,1] = sqrt(mean(nonwildfire_szn_resids^2))
obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[2,2] = max(abs(nonwildfire_szn_resids))
obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[2,3] = median(abs(nonwildfire_szn_resids))

colnames(obs_wildfire_szn_metrics) = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")


pp_rmse = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mar = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mad = numeric(n_samples)

# # Subset statistics
# RMSE_wf = sqrt(mean(resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
# RMSE_nonwf = sqrt(mean(resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))
# 
# MAR_wf = max(abs(resids[wildfire_idx]))
# MAR_nonwf = max(abs(resids[!wildfire_idx]))
# 
# pp_rmse_wf = numeric(n_samples)
# pp_mar_wf = numeric(n_samples)
# 
# pp_rmse_nonwf = numeric(n_samples)
# pp_mar_nonwf = numeric(n_samples)

rep_county_metrics = array(
  NA,
  dim = c(n_samples, 58, 3),
  dimnames = list(
    sample = 1:n_samples,
    county = 1:58,
    metric = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")
  )
)

rep_wildfire_szn_metrics = array(
  NA,
  dim = c(n_samples, 2, 3),
  dimnames = list(
    sample = 1:n_samples,
    wildfire_szn = c(1,0),
    metric = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")
  )
)

#Simulating pp_resids (10 simulations for each obs)
# For each posterior sample
for (j in 1:n_samples){
  y_rep = numeric(length(model3$marginals.fitted.values))
  
  # Sample hyperparameter (size) once per replicate dataset
  n_density = data.frame(model3$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  sample_n = inla.rmarginal(1,inla.smarginal(n_density))
  
  # For each observation
  for (i in 1:length(model3$marginals.fitted.values)){
    row = model3$marginals.fitted.values[[i]] 
    sample_mu = inla.rmarginal(1,inla.smarginal(row))
    y_rep[i] = rnbinom(n = 1, size = sample_n, mu = sample_mu)
  }
  
  # Compute test statistics for this replicate
  # Use the SAME fitted values used for observed residuals
  rep_resids = y_rep - model3$summary.fitted.values$mean
  
  pp_rmse[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids^2))
  pp_mar[j] = max(abs(rep_resids))
  pp_mad[j] = median(abs(rep_resids))
  
  #Stratify results by county 
  for (k in 1:58){
    
    start = (k-1)*72 + 1
    end = k*72
    county_resids = rep_resids[start:end]
    
    rep_county_metrics[j, k, "RMSE"] = sqrt(mean(county_resids^2))
    rep_county_metrics[j, k, "MAR"]  = max(abs(county_resids))
    rep_county_metrics[j, k, "MAD"]  = median(abs(county_resids))
  }
  
  #Stratify results by wildfire szn
  wildfire_szn_resids = rep_resids[wildfire_idx]
  nonwildfire_szn_resids = rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]
  
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 1, "RMSE"] = sqrt(mean(wildfire_szn_resids^2))
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 1, "MAR"]  = max(abs(wildfire_szn_resids))
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 1, "MAD"]  = median(abs(wildfire_szn_resids))
  
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 2, "RMSE"] = sqrt(mean(nonwildfire_szn_resids^2))
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 2, "MAR"]  = max(abs(nonwildfire_szn_resids))
  rep_wildfire_szn_metrics[j, 2, "MAD"]  = median(abs(nonwildfire_szn_resids))
  
  # pp_rmse_wf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
  # pp_mar_wf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]))
  # 
  # pp_rmse_nonwf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))
  # pp_mar_nonwf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]))
}

# Bayesian p-values
p_rmse = mean(pp_rmse >= RMSE)
p_mar = mean(pp_mar >= MAR)
p_mad = mean(pp_mar >= MAD)

cat("Observed RMSE:", RMSE, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated RMSE:", mean(pp_rmse), "\n")
cat("P-value RMSE:", p_rmse, "\n\n")

cat("Observed MAR:", MAR, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAR:", mean(pp_mar), "\n")
cat("P-value MAR:", p_mar, "\n")

cat("Observed MAD:", MAD, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAD:", mean(pp_mad), "\n")
cat("P-value MAD:", p_mad, "\n")

# Visualize
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
hist(pp_rmse, main="Posterior Predictive RMSE", xlab="RMSE")
abline(v=RMSE, col="red", lwd=2)

hist(pp_mar, main="Posterior Predictive MAR", xlab="MAR")
abline(v=MAR, col="red", lwd=2)

hist(pp_mad, main="Posterior Predictive MAD", xlab="MAD")
abline(v=MAD, col="red", lwd=2)



# # Bayesian p-values
# p_rmse_wf = mean(pp_rmse_wf >= RMSE_wf)
# p_mar_wf = mean(pp_mar_wf >= MAR_wf)
# 
# p_rmse_nonwf = mean(pp_rmse_nonwf >= RMSE_nonwf)
# p_mar_nonwf = mean(pp_mar_nonwf >= MAR_nonwf)
# 
# cat('Replicates:',pp_rmse_wf, 'vs. observed:',RMSE_wf)
# cat('Replicates:',pp_mar_wf, 'vs. observed:',MAR_wf)
# 
# cat('Replicates:',pp_rmse_nonwf, 'vs. observed:',RMSE_nonwf)
# cat('Replicates:',pp_mar_nonwf, 'vs. observed:',MAR_nonwf)
# 
# # Visualize
# par(mfrow=c(1,2))
# hist(pp_rmse_nonwf, main="Posterior Predictive RMSE", xlab="RMSE")
# abline(v=RMSE_nonwf, col="red", lwd=2)
# 
# hist(pp_mar_nonwf, main="Posterior Predictive MAR", xlab="MAR")
# abline(v=MAR_nonwf, col="red", lwd=2)
```

Stratifying by county:

```{r}
plot_df <- data.frame(
  value = as.vector(rep_county_metrics),
  replicate = rep(1:n_samples, times = 58 * 3),
  county = rep(rep(counties, each = n_samples), times = 3),
  metric = rep(c("RMSE", "MAR", "MAD"), each = n_samples * 58)
)

plot_df$county = factor(plot_df$county)

county_pops = inla_df %>% group_by(NAME) %>% summarize(avg_pop = mean(Total_Pop))

plot_df$log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop)

# Reorder county factor levels by population size
plot_df$county = factor(plot_df$county, levels = counties[order(county_pops$avg_pop)])
plot_df$log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop[match(plot_df$county, counties)])

observed_df = data.frame(
  log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop),
  observed_rmse = obs_county_metrics[,"RMSE"],
  observed_mar = obs_county_metrics[,"MAR"],
  observed_mad = obs_county_metrics[,"MAD"],
  county = factor(counties, levels = counties[order(county_pops$avg_pop)])
)

#RMSE
rmse_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "RMSE") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = county, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = county, y = observed_rmse), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "County", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive RMSE",
    title = "Posterior Predictive RMSE Distributions by County"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) 

# ggsave(rmse_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Deaths-RMSE-PPC-byCounty.png",dpi = 600)

rmse_plot

#Maximum absolute residual
mar_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "MAR") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = county, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = county, y = observed_mar), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "County", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive MAR",
    title = "Posterior Predictive MAR Distributions by County"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# ggsave(mar_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Deaths-MAR-PPC-byCounty.png",dpi = 600)

mar_plot

#Median absolute deviation
mad_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "MAD") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = county, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = county, y = observed_mad), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "County", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive MAD",
    title = "Posterior Predictive MAD Distributions by County"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# ggsave(mad_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Deaths-MAD-PPC-byCounty.png",dpi = 600)

mad_plot
```

Stratifying by wildfire szn:

```{r}
plot_df <- data.frame(
  value = as.vector(rep_wildfire_szn_metrics),
  replicate = rep(1:n_samples, times = 2 * 3),
  wildfire_szn = rep(rep(c(1,0), each = n_samples), times = 3),
  metric = rep(c("RMSE", "MAR", "MAD"), each = n_samples * 2)
)

plot_df$wildfire_szn = factor(plot_df$wildfire_szn)

observed_df = data.frame(
  observed_rmse = obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[,"RMSE"],
  observed_mar = obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[,"MAR"],
  observed_mad = obs_wildfire_szn_metrics[,"MAD"],
  wildfire_szn = factor(c(1,0))
)

#RMSE
rmse_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "RMSE") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = wildfire_szn, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = wildfire_szn, y = observed_rmse), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Wildfire Season", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive RMSE",
    title = "Posterior Predictive RMSE Distributions by Wildfire Season"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) 

# ggsave(rmse_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Deaths-RMSE-PPC-wildfireszn.png",dpi = 600)

rmse_plot

#Median absolute deviation
mad_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "MAD") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = wildfire_szn, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = wildfire_szn, y = observed_mad), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Wildfire Season", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive MAD",
    title = "Posterior Predictive MAD Distributions by Wildfire Season"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# ggsave(mad_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Deaths-MAD-PPC-wildfireszn.png",dpi = 600)

mad_plot
```

#### PPC on death rate

```{r,cache=TRUE}
n_samples = 100

yobs = inla_df$death_rate
fitted_rate = (model3$summary.fitted.values$mean/inla_df$Total_Pop)*100000
resids = yobs - fitted_rate

RMSE2 = sqrt(mean(resids^2))
MAR2 = max(abs(resids))
MAD2 = median(abs(resids))

obs_county_metrics2 = matrix(NA, nrow = 58, ncol = 3)
colnames(obs_county_metrics2) = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")

for (k in 1:58){
  start = (k-1)*72 + 1
  end = k*72
  county_resids = resids[start:end]

  obs_county_metrics2[k,1] = sqrt(mean(county_resids^2))
  obs_county_metrics2[k,2] = max(abs(county_resids))
  obs_county_metrics2[k,3] = median(abs(county_resids))
}


pp_rmse2 = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mar2 = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mad2 = numeric(n_samples)

# # Subset statistics
# RMSE_wf = sqrt(mean(resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
# RMSE_nonwf = sqrt(mean(resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))
# 
# MAR_wf = max(abs(resids[wildfire_idx]))
# MAR_nonwf = max(abs(resids[!wildfire_idx]))
# 
# pp_rmse_wf = numeric(n_samples)
# pp_mar_wf = numeric(n_samples)
# 
# pp_rmse_nonwf = numeric(n_samples)
# pp_mar_nonwf = numeric(n_samples)

rep_county_metrics2 = array(
  NA,
  dim = c(n_samples, 58, 3),
  dimnames = list(
    sample = 1:n_samples,
    county = 1:58,
    metric = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")
  )
)

#Simulating pp_resids (100 simulations for each obs)
# For each posterior sample
for (j in 1:n_samples){
  y_rep_count = numeric(length(model3$marginals.fitted.values))
  
  # Sample hyperparameter (size) once per replicate dataset
  n_density = data.frame(model3$marginals.hyperpar[[1]])
  sample_n = inla.rmarginal(1,inla.smarginal(n_density))
  
  # For each observation
  for (i in 1:length(model3$marginals.fitted.values)){
    row = model3$marginals.fitted.values[[i]] 
    sample_mu = inla.rmarginal(1,inla.smarginal(row))
    y_rep_count[i] = rnbinom(n = 1, size = sample_n, mu = sample_mu)
  }
  
  # Compute test statistics for this replicate
  # Use the SAME fitted values used for observed residuals
  y_rep_rate = (y_rep_count/inla_df$Total_Pop)*100000
  rep_resids = y_rep_rate - fitted_rate
  
  pp_rmse2[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids^2))
  pp_mar2[j] = max(abs(rep_resids))
  pp_mad2[j] = median(abs(rep_resids))
  
  #Stratify results by county 
  for (k in 1:58){
    
    start = (k-1)*72 + 1
    end = k*72
    county_resids = rep_resids[start:end]
    
    rep_county_metrics2[j, k, "RMSE"] = sqrt(mean(county_resids^2))
    rep_county_metrics2[j, k, "MAR"]  = max(abs(county_resids))
    rep_county_metrics2[j, k, "MAD"]  = median(abs(county_resids))
  }
  
  # pp_rmse_wf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
  # pp_mar_wf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]))
  # 
  # pp_rmse_nonwf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))
  # pp_mar_nonwf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]))
}

# Bayesian p-values
p_rmse2 = mean(pp_rmse2 >= RMSE2)
p_mar2 = mean(pp_mar2 >= MAR2)
p_mad2 = mean(pp_mad2 >= MAD2)

cat("Observed RMSE:", RMSE2, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated RMSE:", mean(pp_rmse2), "\n")
cat("P-value RMSE:", p_rmse2, "\n\n")

cat("Observed MAR:", MAR2, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAR:", mean(pp_mar2), "\n")
cat("P-value MAR:", p_mar2, "\n")

cat("Observed MAD:", MAD2, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAD:", mean(pp_mad2), "\n")
cat("P-value MAD:", p_mad2, "\n")

# Visualize
# par(mfrow=c(1,2))
# hist(pp_rmse2, main="Posterior Predictive RMSE", xlab="RMSE")
# abline(v=RMSE2, col="red", lwd=2)

nb_rmse_df = data.frame(value = pp_rmse2)

nb_rmse_plot <- ggplot(nb_rmse_df, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    bins = 50,
    boundary = 0,
    closed = "left"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = RMSE2,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Negative Binomial RMSE PPC",
    x = "RMSE",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

nb_rmse_plot

# ggsave(nb_rmse_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/NB-Death-Rate-RMSE-PPC.png",dpi = 600)

# hist(pp_mar2, main="Posterior Predictive MAR", xlab="MAR")
# abline(v=MAR2, col="red", lwd=2)

nb_mar_df = data.frame(value = pp_mar2)

nb_mar_plot <- ggplot(nb_mar_df, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    bins = 50,
    boundary = 0,
    closed = "left"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = MAR2,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Negative Binomial MAR PPC",
    x = "MAR",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

nb_mar_plot

# ggsave(nb_mar_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/NB-Death-Rate-MAR-PPC.png",dpi = 600)


# hist(pp_mad2, main="Posterior Predictive MAD", xlab="MAD")
# abline(v=MAD2, col="red", lwd=2)
```

Stratifying by county:

```{r}
plot_df <- data.frame(
  value = as.vector(rep_county_metrics2),
  replicate = rep(1:n_samples, times = 58 * 3),
  county = rep(rep(counties, each = n_samples), times = 3),
  metric = rep(c("RMSE", "MAR", "MAD"), each = n_samples * 58)
)

plot_df$county = factor(plot_df$county)

county_pops = inla_df %>% group_by(NAME) %>% summarize(avg_pop = mean(Total_Pop))

plot_df$log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop)

# Reorder county factor levels by population size
plot_df$county = factor(plot_df$county, levels = counties[order(county_pops$avg_pop)])
plot_df$log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop[match(plot_df$county, counties)])

observed_df = data.frame(
  log_pop = log(county_pops$avg_pop),
  observed_rmse = obs_county_metrics2[,"RMSE"],
  observed_mar = obs_county_metrics2[,"MAR"],
  observed_mad = obs_county_metrics2[,"MAD"],
  county = factor(counties, levels = counties[order(county_pops$avg_pop)])
)


#RMSE
rmse_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "RMSE") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = county, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = county, y = observed_rmse), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "County", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive RMSE",
    title = "Posterior Predictive RMSE Distributions by County"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) 

# ggsave(rmse_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Death-Rate-RMSE-PPC-byCounty.png",dpi = 600)

rmse_plot


#Maximum absolute residual
mar_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "MAR") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = county, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = county, y = observed_mar), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "County", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive MAR",
    title = "Posterior Predictive MAR Distributions by County"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# ggsave(mar_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Death-Rate-MAR-PPC-byCounty.png",dpi = 600)

mar_plot


#Median absolute deviation
mad_plot = plot_df %>% filter(metric == "MAD") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = county, y = value)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5, color = "lightgray", cex = 0.1) + 
  geom_point(data = observed_df, 
             aes(x = county, y = observed_mad), 
             color = "red",size = 1, shape = 16) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "County", 
    y = "Posterior Predictive MAD",
    title = "Posterior Predictive MAD Distributions by County"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```

```{r,eval=TRUE}
rep_county_mean2 = matrix(NA, nrow = 58, ncol = 3)
colnames(rep_county_mean2) = c("RMSE", "MAR", "MAD")

for (m in c("RMSE", "MAR", "MAD")) {
  rep_county_mean2[, m] = apply(
    rep_county_metrics2[, , m],
    MARGIN = 2,          # county dimension
    FUN = mean,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
}

hist(
  rep_county_mean2[, "RMSE"],
  breaks = 15,
  main = "County-level RMSE (Posterior Predictive)",
  xlab = "RMSE",
  col = "grey80",
  border = "white"
)

abline(
  v = RMSE2,
  col = "red",
  lwd = 2
)


hist(
  rep_county_mean2[, "MAR"],
  breaks = 15,
  main = "County-level MAR (Posterior Predictive)",
  xlab = "MAR",
  col = "grey80",
  border = "white"
)

abline(
  v = MAR2,
  col = "red",
  lwd = 2
)


hist(
  rep_county_mean2[, "MAD"],
  breaks = 15,
  main = "County-level MAD (Posterior Predictive)",
  xlab = "MAD",
  col = "grey80",
  border = "white"
)

abline(
  v = MAD2,
  col = "red",
  lwd = 2
)
```

### Poisson PPCs

```{r}
pois_outcome_cpo_vals = pois_model$cpo$cpo
mean(pois_outcome_cpo_vals)

sum(pois_outcome_cpo_vals > 0)  
sum(pois_model$cpo$failure > 0)
bad_obs <- which(pois_outcome_cpo_vals < 0.01)
length(bad_obs)/length(pois_outcome_cpo_vals)
```

```{r}
#PIT
pois_outcome_pit_vals = pois_model$cpo$pit

hist(pois_outcome_pit_vals, breaks=50, probability=TRUE, 
     main="PIT Histogram", xlab="PIT", ylim=c(0,5))
abline(h=1, col="red", lty=2)  # Uniform reference line


df_pit <- data.frame(pit = pois_outcome_pit_vals)


# GGplot PIT histogram
pois_pit_plot <- ggplot(df_pit, aes(x = pit)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    bins = 50,
    boundary = 0,
    closed = "left"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Poisson PIT Histogram",
    x = "PIT",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

pois_pit_plot


ks.test(pois_outcome_pit_vals, "punif")  # Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
```

```{r,cache=TRUE}
n_samples = 100

yobs = inla_df$death_rate
fitted_rate = (pois_model$summary.fitted.values$mean/inla_df$Total_Pop)*100000
resids = yobs - fitted_rate

RMSE2 = sqrt(mean(resids^2))
MAR2 = max(abs(resids))
MAD2 = median(abs(resids))

obs_county_metrics2 = matrix(NA, nrow = 58, ncol = 3)
colnames(obs_county_metrics2) = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")

for (k in 1:58){
  start = (k-1)*72 + 1
  end = k*72
  county_resids = resids[start:end]

  obs_county_metrics2[k,1] = sqrt(mean(county_resids^2))
  obs_county_metrics2[k,2] = max(abs(county_resids))
  obs_county_metrics2[k,3] = median(abs(county_resids))
}


pp_rmse2 = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mar2 = numeric(n_samples)
pp_mad2 = numeric(n_samples)

# # Subset statistics
# RMSE_wf = sqrt(mean(resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
# RMSE_nonwf = sqrt(mean(resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))
# 
# MAR_wf = max(abs(resids[wildfire_idx]))
# MAR_nonwf = max(abs(resids[!wildfire_idx]))
# 
# pp_rmse_wf = numeric(n_samples)
# pp_mar_wf = numeric(n_samples)
# 
# pp_rmse_nonwf = numeric(n_samples)
# pp_mar_nonwf = numeric(n_samples)

rep_county_metrics2 = array(
  NA,
  dim = c(n_samples, 58, 3),
  dimnames = list(
    sample = 1:n_samples,
    county = 1:58,
    metric = c("RMSE","MAR","MAD")
  )
)

#Simulating pp_resids (100 simulations for each obs)
# For each posterior sample
for (j in 1:n_samples){
  y_rep_count = numeric(length(pois_model$marginals.fitted.values))
  
  for (i in 1:length(pois_model$marginals.fitted.values)){
    row = data.frame(pois_model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])
  
    sample_lambda = inla.rmarginal(1,inla.smarginal(row))
    mean = mean(sample_lambda)
  
    y_rep_count[i] = rpois(n=1,lambda = sample_lambda)
}
  
  # Compute test statistics for this replicate
  # Use the SAME fitted values used for observed residuals
  y_rep_rate = (y_rep_count/inla_df$Total_Pop)*100000
  rep_resids = y_rep_rate - fitted_rate
  
  pp_rmse2[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids^2))
  pp_mar2[j] = max(abs(rep_resids))
  pp_mad2[j] = median(abs(rep_resids))
  
  #Stratify results by county 
  # for (k in 1:58){
  #   
  #   start = (k-1)*72 + 1
  #   end = k*72
  #   county_resids = rep_resids[start:end]
  #   
  #   rep_county_metrics2[j, k, "RMSE"] = sqrt(mean(county_resids^2))
  #   rep_county_metrics2[j, k, "MAR"]  = max(abs(county_resids))
  #   rep_county_metrics2[j, k, "MAD"]  = median(abs(county_resids))
  # }
  
  # pp_rmse_wf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]^2))
  # pp_mar_wf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[wildfire_idx]))
  # 
  # pp_rmse_nonwf[j] = sqrt(mean(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]^2))
  # pp_mar_nonwf[j] = max(abs(rep_resids[!wildfire_idx]))
}

# Bayesian p-values
p_rmse2 = mean(pp_rmse2 >= RMSE2)
p_mar2 = mean(pp_mar2 >= MAR2)
p_mad2 = mean(pp_mad2 >= MAD2)

cat("Observed RMSE:", RMSE2, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated RMSE:", mean(pp_rmse2), "\n")
cat("P-value RMSE:", p_rmse2, "\n\n")

cat("Observed MAR:", MAR2, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAR:", mean(pp_mar2), "\n")
cat("P-value MAR:", p_mar2, "\n")

cat("Observed MAD:", MAD2, "\n")
cat("Mean replicated MAD:", mean(pp_mad2), "\n")
cat("P-value MAD:", p_mad2, "\n")

# Visualize
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
hist(pp_rmse2, main="Posterior Predictive RMSE", xlab="RMSE")
abline(v=RMSE2, col="red", lwd=2)

pois_rmse_df = data.frame(value = pp_rmse2)

pois_rmse_plot <- ggplot(pois_rmse_df, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    bins = 50,
    boundary = 0,
    closed = "left"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = RMSE2,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Poisson RMSE PPC",
    x = "RMSE",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

pois_rmse_plot

# ggsave(pois_rmse_plot,filename = "~/work/important-plots/Poisson-Death-Rate-RMSE-PPC.png",dpi = 600)

# hist(pp_mar2, main="Posterior Predictive MAR", xlab="MAR")
# abline(v=MAR2, col="red", lwd=2)

pois_mar_df = data.frame(value = pp_mar2)

pois_mar_plot <- ggplot(pois_mar_df, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    bins = 50,
    boundary = 0,
    closed = "left"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = MAR2,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Poisson MAR PPC",
    x = "MAR",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

pois_mar_plot
```

### Sensitivity analysis for unmeasured confounding

```{r,cache=TRUE}
#Create negative control proxies (Z = future exposure, W = past outcome)
inla_df <- inla_df %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  mutate(
    W1 = lag(deaths, 1),
    W4 = lag(deaths, 3),
    W8 = lag(deaths, 6),
    W12 = lag(deaths, 12),
    Zp1 = lead(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,1),
    Zp4 = lead(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,3),
    Zp8 = lead(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,6),
    Zp12 = lead(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,12)
  ) %>% ungroup()

#Fit model with proxies to see if nonexistent associations appear 
kgr_formula_hierarchical_wproxy = W1 ~ offset(log(Total_Pop/100000)) +
  factor(month) +
  f(time,model = "iid",hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec",param = c(0.5,0.01))),constr=TRUE) + 
  sw_prod2 + I(sw_prod2^2) + I(sw_prod2^3) +
  f(NAME, model = "iid",constr=TRUE) + 
  ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,df=3) + Zp1 + 
  f(pm25_dev, model = "rw2", replicate = Group, scale.model = TRUE, constr=TRUE,
    hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(0.05, 0.5)))) +
  f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), 
    group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))

proxy_model = inla(formula = kgr_formula_hierarchical_wproxy,family = "nbinomial",data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

proxy_model$summary.fixed
proxy_model$summary.hyperpar
```

### FOR REFERENCE:

SKATER clusters by SDI:

```{r}
#detach("package:MASS", unload = TRUE) 

clusterlabels$Cluster = factor(clusterlabels$Cluster)
clusterlabels$subregion = tolower(clusterlabels$counties)

merged_data <- merge(ca_map, clusterlabels, by = "subregion", all.x = TRUE)

# Define distinct colors for each category
#distinct_colors = qualitative_hcl(7,palette = "dynamic")
distinct_colors <- c("red", "blue", "green", "purple", "yellow")
# distinct_colors <- c("red", "blue")

#Plot
gg_with_grouping <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data = merged_data, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group, fill = Cluster), 
               color = "black") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.3, xlim = c(-125, -112), ylim = c(30, 42)) +
  theme_void() +
  scale_color_viridis(discrete=TRUE) +
  theme(
      title = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.text  = element_text(size = 20),  # legend labels
      legend.title = element_text(size = 20)   # legend title
    )

gg_with_grouping
```

SDI groups (into quintiles):

```{r}
#Attach group labels to each county
group_labels$Group = factor(group_labels$Group)
group_labels$subregion = tolower(group_labels$counties)

merged_data <- merge(ca_map, group_labels, by = "subregion", all.x = TRUE)

# Define distinct colors for each category
#distinct_colors = qualitative_hcl(7,palette = "dynamic")
distinct_colors <- c("red", "blue", "green", "purple", "yellow")
# distinct_colors <- c("red", "blue")

#Plot
gg_with_grouping <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data = merged_data, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group, fill = Group), 
               color = "black") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.3, xlim = c(-125, -112), ylim = c(30, 42)) +
  theme_void() +
  scale_color_viridis(discrete=TRUE) +
  theme(
      title = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.text  = element_text(size = 20),  # legend labels
      legend.title = element_text(size = 20)   # legend title
    )

gg_with_grouping
```

```{r}
county_stats <- inla_df %>%
  group_by(NAME) %>%
  summarize(
    n_obs = n(),
    mean_exposure = mean(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2),
    sd_exposure = sd(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2),
    cv_exposure = sd_exposure / mean_exposure,
    zero_prop = mean(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 == 0),
    max_exposure = max(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2)
  )
```

# What is DRF? DRF models the expected value of potential outcomes Y as a function of the treatment level and covariates

## Example with z = 100: We set z = 100 for all observations and predict what the potential outcome would be for each county-month

```{r,eval=FALSE}
#Attach duplicate dataset with specified treatment/dose level 
duplicate_df = inla_df
# duplicate_df = inla_df %>% filter(NAME %in% counties[16:30])
# duplicate_df = inla_df %>% filter(NAME == "Kings")

duplicate_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2_binned = 100
duplicate_df$deaths = NA 

inla_cf_df = rbind(inla_df,duplicate_df)
cf_idx = c((nrow(inla_df)+1):nrow(inla_cf_df))

# Same formula as above
kgr_formula_hierarchical = deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop/100000)) +
  factor(month) +
  sw_prod2 + I(sw_prod2^2) + I(sw_prod2^3) +
  f(NAME, model = "iid") + 
  ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,df=3) + 
  f(pm25_dev, model = "rw2", replicate = Group, scale.model = TRUE,
    hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(0.05, 0.5)))) +
  f(ID, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = solve(H^2), 
    group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar", order = 6))

model = inla(formula = kgr_formula_hierarchical,family = "nbinomial",data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))



###Extract relevant information and store in the list
model_summary <- model$summary.fixed
bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model)
model_DIC <- model$dic$dic
model_WAIC <- model$waic$waic
preds_model <- model$summary.fitted.values
preds_model <- cbind(inla_cf_df$NAME, inla_cf_df$time, inla_cf_df$date, inla_cf_df$deaths, preds_model)
colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "date", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

a = preds_model[cf_idx,]
mean(a$mean)

# a = preds_model[cf_idx,] %>% filter(county == "Kings")
# mean(a$mean)
```

# ADRF: average of DRF over all individuals in the population

Load data to start causal analysis: 

```{r,eval=FALSE}
#load("P2-Causal-Analysis-NB-totalcum2-1.14.RData")
load("~/work/causal-wildfire-smoke-2-duplicate/P2-Causal-Analysis-NB-totalcum2-2.7.RData") 
inla_df$Group = factor(inla_df$Group)
```

## Defining dose grid: 

```{r}
# dose_grid = c(0,0.1,0.5,1,5,10,50,100,500,1000) #cum2
p = c(seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.1), 0.95)

cum2_quantiles = quantile(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2,probs = p)
# cum2_quantiles = quantile(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2[inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_2 > 0],probs = p)
print(cum2_quantiles)

dose_grid = round(cum2_quantiles,3)

exposure_label = expression(paste("2 month accumulation of smoke PM2.5 ("*mu*"g/m"^3*")"))
```

## Function to calculate ADRFs for grid of treatment values

```{r}
DRF_to_ADRF = function(DRF_list,dataset){
  ADRF = data.frame(Z = dose_grid, mu_hat = NA, weighted_mu_hat = NA, rate_hat = NA, weighted_rate_hat = NA)
  county_pops = dataset %>% select(NAME,date,Total_Pop) 

  for (i in 1:length(DRF_list)){
    dose_results = DRF_list[[i]]
    dose_results_pop = dose_results %>% left_join(county_pops,by = c("county" = "NAME","date" = "date"))
    
    # Calculate population-weighted mean
    total_pop = sum(dose_results_pop$Total_Pop)
    pop_weights = dose_results_pop$Total_Pop / total_pop
    
    ADRF$mu_hat[i] = mean(dose_results_pop$mean)
    ADRF$rate_hat[i] = sum(dose_results_pop$mean)/total_pop*100000 # avg individual level rate
  }

  return(ADRF)
}

collect_bootstrap_results = function(bootstrap_results){
  DRF_list = list()
  
  for (i in 1:length(dose_grid)){
    dose_results = bootstrap_results[[1]][[i]]
    
    for (j in 2:length(bootstrap_results)){
      dose_results = rbind(dose_results,bootstrap_results[[j]][[i]])
    }
    
    
    ###FILTER OUT BAD BOOTSTRAP SAMPLES (samples or just predictions???)
    bad_preds = which(dose_results$mean >= 10000)

    if(length(bad_preds) > 0){
      bad_sims = unique(dose_results$sample_id[bad_preds])
      good_idx = which(!(dose_results$sample_id %in% bad_sims))
      dose_results = dose_results[good_idx,]
    }
    
    DRF_list[[i]] = dose_results
  }
  
  return(DRF_list)
}

DRF_to_ADRF2 = function(DRF_list, dataset) {
  ADRF = data.frame(
    Z = dose_grid, 
    # Unweighted estimates
    mu_hat = NA, mu_hat_lower = NA, mu_hat_upper = NA, 
    rate_hat = NA, rate_hat_lower = NA, rate_hat_upper = NA
  )
  
  county_pops = dataset %>% select(NAME, date, Total_Pop) 
  
  for (i in 1:length(DRF_list)) {
    
    dose_results = DRF_list[[i]]
    dose_results_pop = dose_results %>% 
      left_join(county_pops, by = c("county" = "NAME", "date" = "date"))
    
    # Calculate both weighted and unweighted estimates for each sample
    dose_summary = dose_results_pop %>% 
      group_by(sample_id) %>% 
      summarize(
        # Unweighted estimates
        sample_mu_hat = mean(mean),
        sample_rate_hat = sum(mean)/sum(Total_Pop)*100000,
        .groups = "drop"
      )
    
    # Calculate credible intervals for unweighted estimates
    ADRF$mu_hat[i] = mean(dose_summary$sample_mu_hat)
    ADRF$mu_hat_lower[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_mu_hat, 0.025)
    ADRF$mu_hat_upper[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_mu_hat, 0.975)
    
    ADRF$rate_hat[i] = mean(dose_summary$sample_rate_hat)
    ADRF$rate_hat_lower[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_rate_hat, 0.025)
    ADRF$rate_hat_upper[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_rate_hat, 0.975)
  }
  
  return(ADRF)
}

# parallel_bootstrap_test_results = readRDS("~/work/cum2-bootstrap-samples/parallel_bootstrap_test_results-12.8.rds")

# parallel_bootstrap_test_results = readRDS("~/work/causal-wildfire-smoke-2-duplicate/cum2-bootstrap-samples/parallel_bootstrap_test_results-2.13.rds") #new model + ili prevalence in PS model

parallel_bootstrap_test_results = readRDS("~/work/causal-wildfire-smoke-2-duplicate/cum2-bootstrap-samples/parallel_bootstrap_test_results-2.22.rds") #new model + ili prevalence in PS model

# parallel_bootstrap_test_results = readRDS("~/work/causal-wildfire-smoke-2-duplicate/cum2-bootstrap-samples/parallel_bootstrap_test_results-2.28.rds") #new model + ili prevalence in PS model

bootstrapped_DRFs = collect_bootstrap_results(parallel_bootstrap_test_results)
full_data_ADRF = DRF_to_ADRF2(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df)
```

```{r,eval=FALSE}
    error = function(e) {
```
